Summary

Responsible for overseeing the successful implementation and data migration of payroll systems for Deel’s clients. You will lead and manage the full payroll implementation process, ensuring that clients’ data is accurately transferred, systems are properly configured, and the transition is seamless. You’ll collaborate closely with internal teams and external stakeholders, guiding clients through the setup and migration phases, while offering ongoing support and training.

Responsibilities

  • Project Set-up – work with our onboarding team and clients to organize and run kick off meetings, and deliver our implementation. Create, maintain and manage project plans to deliver the successful roll-out of clients.

  • Client and Project Management – Support project review calls (internal and external), coordinating and delivering on the project streams. Refer and manage any changes to the scope of work and delivery schedule.

  • Payroll Process Requirements – Define and set-up the agreed client payroll processes, establishing all of the payroll and data requirements. Ensure each client payroll calendar is managed timely and effectively.

  • Documentation - Maintain detailed project documentation, including project plans, status reports, and process documentation.

  • System set-up and parallel payroll process – define, agree and configure all interfaces, reports and systems configurations to successfully deliver the parallel and live payroll for month 1 and 2, if applicable.

  • Go-live transition, Sign-off and documentation – Work with the client to sign-off on the project and document all of the client specific needs and processes ensuring validation and confirmation on the project implementation. Responsible for generating reports to analyze, audit, and reconcile payroll data.

  • Reports- Mapping of GL reports if required

  • Communication – Act as the point of contact for all payroll project matters for the client.

  • The role is primarily autonomous and will set priorities and manage the project appropriately. Regular status reporting to senior leadership is required, with this role taking ownership for appropriately escalating issues where necessary. Schedule and attend weekly client calls to report on project status, identifying any risks, actions, issues and dependencies

  • Project Management - Lead and manage the end-to-end implementation of a global payroll system, ensuring on-time and on-budget delivery.

  • Client Management - Gather key requirements from clients to facilitate the onboarding process, and guide them through the technical payroll aspects of onboarding in a professional, clear manner.

  • Requirements Gathering - Collaborate with HR, Finance, and other stakeholders to gather payroll requirements

  • Compliance - Ensure that all payroll processes adhere to local tax and labor laws, keeping up-to-date with any changes.

  • Data Migration - Oversee the migration of payroll data from legacy systems to the new global payroll system, ensuring data accuracy and security.

  • Testing - Develop and execute a comprehensive testing plan to identify and resolve system issues, ensuring a smooth transition to the new payroll system.

  • Change Management - Implement change management strategies to ensure a seamless transition for employees and HR teams.

Qualifications

  • 5+ years of experience in global payroll implementation and payroll operations.

  • Strong knowledge of payroll processes and compliance across your designated location.

  • Experience with payroll systems and software.

  • Client-facing project management experience and ability to manage multiple projects.

  • Project management certification (e.g., PMP) is a plus.

  • Excellent communication, collaboration, and problem-solving skills.

  • Ability to manage multiple projects under tight deadlines.

  • Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business, or related field (Master’s preferred).

Team Lead

About the Team
At the core of Deel’s FinTech platform, responsible for building and operating client and contractor wallets across our regulated entities globally. This is a mission-critical domain where accuracy, compliance, and reliability are non-negotiable.

Scope & Responsibilities

  • Lead the fintech engineering team to design, build, and scale wallet infrastructure for global deel's users.

  • Work with different teams to integrate wallets with regional banks through PSPs (via APIs), orchestrating pay-ins and ensuring seamless treasury operations.

  • Depending on the project, you may collaborate with the Core Accounting team to connect wallets with the General Ledger (GL), ensuring balances are accurate and financial records meet audit standards.

  • Work closely with Compliance, Treasury, and PSP Integration teams to ensure adherence to local regulations (MTL, PI, safeguarding requirements).

  • Provide technical leadership in a global, fast-paced environment where uptime and precision are critical.

  • Responsible for leading a high-performing engineering team, including running daily stand-ups, planning sessions, and backlog grooming.

What We’re Looking For

  • Location: Based in EMEA, with high availability for global collaboration.

  • Background: Proven experience as a Team Lead or Manager in payments, banking, accounting, or treasury.

  • FinTech Expertise: Hands-on wallets, flows of funds, compliance, and regulated money movement. Prior experience at companies like Wise, PayPal, or similar is a strong plus.

  • Technical Skills:

    • JavaScript/Node.js

    • PostgreSQL with Sequelize ORM

    • Strong understanding of distributed systems and API-driven banking/PSP integrations

  • Leadership: Ability to run projects at scale, mentor engineers, and drive cross-functional alignment.

  • Mindset: Ownership, detail-oriented, and mindful of the responsibility of managing other people’s money.

  • Important note: This role will remain technically hands-on, with responsibilities divided between people management and hands-on tasks.

Why This Role Matters
This team is the foundation of Deel’s regulated money movement. The work ensures client funds are safe, compliant, and seamlessly accessible worldwide

Summary

The Sales Development Representative plays a critical role in driving top-of-funnel growth across a defined segment or product. This role is responsible for identifying, engaging, and qualifying prospective customers through inbound and outbound prospecting. By uncovering client needs, educating prospects, and generating qualified opportunities, the SDR directly contributes to pipeline development and overall revenue performance. The position requires strong communication skills, curiosity, and the ability to operate in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

Responsibilities

  • Generate new business opportunities through inbound and outbound prospecting, including email, phone, social channels, events, and campaigns.

  • Identify, research, and qualify prospective clients by understanding their needs, challenges, and readiness to engage with sales.

  • Create and execute outreach sequences, including personalized email campaigns and cold-calling to map and penetrate accounts.

  • Engage existing customers as needed to identify cross-sell or expansion opportunities across a defined segment or product (as applicable to the team).

  • Educate and nurture leads to progress them toward a qualified meeting or next step in the buyer journey.

  • Maintain accurate and up-to-date lead and activity data in CRM and sales engagement tools.

  • Collaborate with Account Executives to ensure smooth and effective handoff of qualified opportunities.

  • Partner with Sales, Marketing, and other teams to continuously refine lead-generation processes, messaging, and workflow improvements.

  • Achieve or exceed monthly and quarterly targets for qualified meetings, Sales Qualified Opportunities, and pipeline contribution.

  • Participate in ongoing training, coaching, and skill development.

Qualifications

  • Experience in a sales development, business development, or outbound commercial role (experience level may vary depending on team needs).

  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage prospects across multiple channels.

  • Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, dynamic, and remote-friendly environment.

  • Highly organized, self-motivated, and able to manage multiple priorities and outreach activities simultaneously.

  • Proven track record of meeting or exceeding activity and pipeline-generation targets.

  • Comfortable working autonomously, taking initiative, and sourcing solutions independently.

  • Experience with CRM systems and sales engagement tools is a plus.

  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a strong growth orientation; adaptable to changes in process, product, or market focus.

  • Strong written communication skills, including the ability to craft compelling outreach messages and campaign sequences.

  • Experience in HR tech, fintech, or high-growth startup environments is beneficial but not required.

As the Growth Marketing Science Lead, you will be the architect of our "Test and Learn" ecosystem.

With an annual spend exceeding $100M, your mission is to ensure every dollar is optimized through rigorous statistical validation. You will translate complex data into high-growth strategies for our search and paid social teams, ensuring we aren't just spending, but scaling intelligently.

Responsibilities

  • Experimentation Roadmap: Design and lead a high-velocity A/B and multivariate testing roadmap across channels, campaign types, ad creatives and audience segments.

  • Strategic Guidance: Serve as the "strategic pulse" for the Paid Marketing team. Provide data-backed briefs that tell Channel Managers exactly where to pivot spend based on test winners.

  • Opportunity Discovery: Go beyond standard reporting to "mine" data for untapped growth levers. Identify patterns that haven't been scaled and propose the business case for expansion.

  • Platform Alpha & Best Practices: Stay at the bleeding edge of ad platform capabilities (Google, Meta, LinkedIn). Experiment with "alpha/beta" features (e.g., Value-Based Bidding, PMax experimentation) to ensure our stack is consistently ahead of industry benchmarks.

  • Advanced Attribution: Continuously refine our multi-touch attribution (MTA) and Media Mix Modeling (MMM) models to measure the incremental impact of non-linear B2B buyer journeys.

  • Marketing Data Infrastructure: Partner with Analytics Engineering to build clean, scalable data models in Snowflake (via dbt) that specifically track experiment lift and incrementalities, and present their performance metrics in dashboard.

Desired Skills and Competencies

  • 5+ years in a Data Analytics or Growth Marketing role, ideally within a high-spend B2B environment.

  • Proficiency in SQL, Looker, Snowflake or comparable stack.

  • Deep understanding of experimentation methodology, sample size calculation, p-values and confidence intervals.

  • Familiarity with the B2B sales funnel

  • A "growth mindset" - curious and eager to learn

We are seeking an M&A Integration Specialist to support Deel’s strategic growth through acquisitions and post-transaction integration. This role combines financial modeling, valuation analysis, and operational execution to evaluate acquisition opportunities and ensure successful integration into Deel’s financial, regulatory, and operational infrastructure.

Given Deel’s global fintech platform, the role requires the ability to assess transactions across multiple dimensions including tax structures, payment flows, regulatory frameworks, and legal considerations.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Evaluate potential acquisition opportunities aligned with Deel’s strategic objectives

  • Build and maintain financial models to assess transaction economics and investment returns

  • Perform company valuation analysis including DCF, comparable company analysis, precedent transactions, and football field valuations

  • Support due diligence processes including financial, operational, regulatory, tax, and legal analysis

  • Analyze target company financial statements, revenue models, and cost structures

  • Prepare internal investment materials, financial analyses, and decision-support presentations for leadership

  • Assess cross-border tax structures, payments infrastructure, regulatory licensing requirements, and legal entity implications of potential acquisitions

  • Coordinate closely with Finance, Tax, Legal, Compliance, Payments, and Product teams during transaction evaluation

  • Support negotiation and structuring of acquisition transactions in collaboration with internal and external advisors

  • Develop integration plans for acquired businesses across financial reporting, systems, and operational processes

  • Align acquired entities with Deel’s accounting policies, financial reporting standards, and internal controls

  • Support integration of financial systems, ERP structures, and reporting processes

  • Monitor post-acquisition integration progress and identify operational or financial risks

  • Identify process improvements and operational synergies following acquisitions

  • Maintain documentation and analysis supporting strategic transaction decisions

Qualifications:

  • 4–7 years of experience in M&A, corporate development, investment banking, or transaction advisory

  • Strong financial modeling and valuation experience

  • Experience building DCF models, multiples analysis, precedent transaction analysis, and football field valuations

  • Understanding of cross-border tax considerations, payments infrastructure, and regulatory frameworks

  • Legal and tax background is preferred; CPA qualification is a strong advantage

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities

  • Ability to manage complex cross-functional projects

  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills

The Sales Development Representative plays a critical role in driving top-of-funnel growth across a defined segment or product. This role is responsible for identifying, engaging, and qualifying prospective customers through inbound and outbound prospecting. By uncovering client needs, educating prospects, and generating qualified opportunities, the SDR directly contributes to pipeline development and overall revenue performance. The position requires strong communication skills, curiosity, and the ability to operate in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

Responsibilities

  • Generate new business opportunities through inbound and outbound prospecting, including email, phone, social channels, events, and campaigns.

  • Identify, research, and qualify prospective clients by understanding their needs, challenges, and readiness to engage with sales.

  • Create and execute outreach sequences, including personalized email campaigns and cold-calling to map and penetrate accounts.

  • Engage existing customers as needed to identify cross-sell or expansion opportunities across a defined segment or product (as applicable to the team).

  • Educate and nurture leads to progress them toward a qualified meeting or next step in the buyer journey.

  • Maintain accurate and up-to-date lead and activity data in CRM and sales engagement tools.

  • Collaborate with Account Executives to ensure smooth and effective handoff of qualified opportunities.

  • Partner with Sales, Marketing, and other teams to continuously refine lead-generation processes, messaging, and workflow improvements.

  • Achieve or exceed monthly and quarterly targets for qualified meetings, Sales Qualified Opportunities, and pipeline contribution.

  • Participate in ongoing training, coaching, and skill development.

Qualifications

  • Experience in a sales development, business development, or outbound commercial role (experience level may vary depending on team needs).

  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage prospects across multiple channels.

  • Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, dynamic, and remote-friendly environment.

  • Highly organized, self-motivated, and able to manage multiple priorities and outreach activities simultaneously.

  • Proven track record of meeting or exceeding activity and pipeline-generation targets.

  • Comfortable working autonomously, taking initiative, and sourcing solutions independently.

  • Experience with CRM systems and sales engagement tools is a plus.

  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a strong growth orientation; adaptable to changes in process, product, or market focus.

  • Strong written communication skills, including the ability to craft compelling outreach messages and campaign sequences.

  • Experience in HR tech, fintech, or high-growth startup environments is beneficial but not required.

As Deel continues to scale rapidly, Employer of Record (EOR) has become the largest and most foundational business within the company, representing roughly one-third of our total revenue and serving as the engine behind Deel’s global expansion platform.

We are seeking a General Manager to lead the EOR business end-to-end. This leader will own the strategy, growth trajectory, and operational excellence of our largest business unit, ensuring it continues to scale globally while expanding into new verticals and industries.

The Opportunity

EOR sits at the center of Deel’s mission: enabling companies to build global teams without friction. The business is already operating at a significant scale and continues to grow rapidly.This role will own the EOR P&L and growth strategy, with a mandate to accelerate expansion while evolving the product and operational model to support increasingly complex global use cases.

A key focus will be verticalizing the people offering, tailoring EOR solutions for industries with unique regulatory, operational, and workforce requirements—such as shipping, mining, entertainment, and other complex global sectors.

The General Manager will work cross-functionally across product, engineering, legal, operations, finance, and go-to-market teams to drive growth, product innovation, and operational scalability.

This is a hands-on leadership role for a business builder who can operate at both the strategic and operational levels—setting direction while rolling up their sleeves to drive execution.

What You’ll Do

  • Own the EOR business end-to-end with full P&L, growth strategy, and global operating performance.

  • Accelerate growth, define and execute the roadmap to expand market leadership.

  • Drive verticalization, build industry-specific EOR solutions (e.g., shipping, mining, entertainment).

  • Scale operations, oversee global compliance, onboarding, payroll, and lifecycle; build for efficiency and scale.

  • Lead cross-functional execution. align Product, Engineering, GTM, Finance, and Legal around clear priorities.

  • Evolve the product toward a more scalable, tech-enabled platform (automation, APIs, CX).

  • Operate with data to prioritize investments and improve performance.

  • Act as the face of EORinternally and externally with key stakeholders.

What We’re Looking For

  • 15+ years experience in GM, operations, or workforce platforms.

  • Proven track record owning and scaling large P&L businesses in high-growth environments.

  • Experience in global, regulated, and operationally complex businesses.

  • Ability to lead large, matrixed organizations and influence without authority.

  • Strategic + hands-on operator with strong execution rigor.

  • Excellent problem-solving, prioritization, and decision-making skills.

  • Strong product and operational judgment.

  • Executive presence with C-suite credibility.

Nice to Have

  • Background in HR tech, payroll, or workforce infrastructure.

  • Experience scaling global, regulated platforms or services businesses.

  • Track record of expanding into new verticals.

  • Exposure to global compliance and employment frameworks.

Summary

Global Mobility Manager - Field Services partners directly with Deel’s go-to-market and internal teams to drive customer success. In this role, you’ll work hand-in-hand with Account Executives to support prospective clients as they evaluate Deel’s platform and product offerings specifically in the field services and onsite space—playing an essential part in showcasing value, credibility, and technical fit.

You’ll be instrumental in translating complex client needs into actionable, scalable, and compliant solutions—supporting every stage of the sales process from discovery through to implementation hand-off.


Responsibilities

  • Lead as the technical authority for Deel’s Field Services segment, driving enterprise pre-sales across staffing, RPO, and project-based organizations with credibility and industry expertise.

  • Own the end-to-end technical sales cycle—leading discovery, shaping solutions, responding to RFPs, delivering tailored demos, and running architecture workshops.

  • Translate complex, multi-jurisdictional requirements into clear, scalable, and compliant solution designs with defined implementation roadmaps and success metrics.

  • Partner with Product, Engineering, and Operations to resolve technical challenges, advocate for customer needs, and accelerate deal progression.

  • Build, refine, and scale a field service-specific pre-sales asset library, including demo narratives, compliance frameworks, workforce models, and objection-handling playbooks.

  • Maintain deep expertise in global employment regulations, immigration shifts, and offshore workforce trends to ensure real-world relevance.

  • Advise customers on structuring compliant workforce models, including secondments, shadow payroll, and offshore/rotational employment frameworks.

Qualifications

  • Minimum 7+ years of deep, practical knowledge of the field services industry is essential, gained through direct experience working within or alongside offshore, onshore, or project-based workforce environments. Candidates must bring firsthand understanding of how these workforces are structured, deployed, and managed.

  • Demonstrated understanding of different offshore employment models and able to advise customers on how to design compliant, commercially sound workforce arrangements across multiple jurisdictions.

  • Mobility or Commercial or Tax exposure is an added bonus.

  • Willingness to travel inside and outside of territory (~10% or less).

  • Outstanding presentation and storytelling skills, always focusing on customer value (multilingual capability is a bonus).

Summary

Spearhead all financial activities within South Africa. This pivotal role requires a qualified accountant with experience in managing accounting, tax and financial aspects, essential for scaling our business effectively in the country.

Responsibilities

  • Oversee and develop all financial activities in the country, including accounting, tax compliance, and financial reporting with improvements to period end closing a focus.

  • Prepare and manage ledger entries, payroll, tax benefits, and contributions reports for regulatory bodies such as social security tax authorities, insurance, pension companies, and others.

  • Reconcile all balance sheet accounts, including (Intercompany) banking, customers, vendors, and accruals, ensuring accuracy and compliance.

  • Monitor day-to-day transactions, maintain up-to-date books, and prepare comprehensive financial and administrative reports as required.

  • Ensure accuracy in payroll calculations made by our local Payroll Manager, oversee payroll software selection and integration, and address any issues related to payroll information and reporting.

  • Minimum of 5+ years as an accountant in high-tech companies, with a robust understanding of financial management and compliance.

  • Proficiency in managing Payroll ERP systems is preferred, with experience in Netsuite being advantageous.

  • Significant experience working in a global environment, understanding diverse financial regulations and practices.

  • Strong Excel and office skills are essential for data analysis and reporting.

  • Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English are required as well as local languages.

The Role: Partner Manager (LATAM – Strategic & Ecosystem Partnerships)

We are looking for a strategic and commercially driven Partner Manager to build and scale Deel’s partner ecosystem across LATAM, with a focus on professional services and strategic partners such as financial institutions, fintech/crypto platforms, cloud providers, and talent partners. This role combines new partner development with long-term relationship ownership. You will identify, sign, and activate high-impact partnerships, while building deep, executive-level relationships that drive mutual growth and long-term value.

You will play a key role in embedding Deel into the broader business ecosystem—making Deel a core infrastructure partner for companies operating globally.

What You’ll Do

  • Identify, source, and close new strategic partnerships across banks, fintech and crypto platforms, cloud providers, talent partners, and other ecosystem players, engaging senior stakeholders through signed agreements

  • Lead partner onboarding, activation, and early-stage success to ensure strong launches and long-term viability

  • Build and manage executive-level relationships with key partners in LATAM, positioning Deel as the preferred global hiring and payroll partner within their offerings

  • Develop and execute joint business plans and go-to-market strategies with partners to drive mutual growth

  • Own pipeline creation and revenue generation from partner channels, including co-selling and cross-selling opportunities

  • Build scalable, repeatable motions that convert partnerships into consistent and predictable revenue streams

  • Expand Deel’s presence across key ecosystems (financial services, Web3/crypto, cloud, HR/talent), representing the company at industry events and strengthening brand positioning

  • Collaborate cross-functionally with Partner Operations, Partner Growth, Sales, Marketing, and Product teams, while tracking performance metrics (activation, engagement, pipeline, revenue) to optimize partnership strategy and inform go-to-market decisions

What We’re Looking For

  • 5+ years of experience in partnerships, business development, or channel roles

  • Proven experience sourcing, negotiating, and closing new partnerships

  • Strong track record of driving pipeline and revenue through partners

  • Experience working with or selling into professional services ecosystems (e.g., financial institutions, fintech, crypto, cloud, HR/talent partners)

  • Ability to build and manage executive-level relationships

  • Strong understanding of the LATAM market and business landscape

  • Excellent communication and storytelling skills

  • Self-motivated, proactive, and comfortable in a fast-paced, high-growth environment

  • Strong cross-functional collaborator

  • Fluency in English, Spanish, or Portuguese

Summary

Global Mobility Manager - Field Services partners directly with Deel’s go-to-market and internal teams to drive customer success. In this role, you’ll work hand-in-hand with Account Executives to support prospective clients as they evaluate Deel’s platform and product offerings specifically in the field services and onsite space—playing an essential part in showcasing value, credibility, and technical fit.

You’ll be instrumental in translating complex client needs into actionable, scalable, and compliant solutions—supporting every stage of the sales process from discovery through to implementation hand-off.


Responsibilities

  • Lead as the technical authority for Deel’s Field Services segment, driving enterprise pre-sales across staffing, RPO, and project-based organizations with credibility and industry expertise.

  • Own the end-to-end technical sales cycle—leading discovery, shaping solutions, responding to RFPs, delivering tailored demos, and running architecture workshops.

  • Translate complex, multi-jurisdictional requirements into clear, scalable, and compliant solution designs with defined implementation roadmaps and success metrics.

  • Partner with Product, Engineering, and Operations to resolve technical challenges, advocate for customer needs, and accelerate deal progression.

  • Build, refine, and scale a field service-specific pre-sales asset library, including demo narratives, compliance frameworks, workforce models, and objection-handling playbooks.

  • Maintain deep expertise in global employment regulations, immigration shifts, and offshore workforce trends to ensure real-world relevance.

  • Advise customers on structuring compliant workforce models, including secondments, shadow payroll, and offshore/rotational employment frameworks.

Qualifications

  • Minimum 7+ years of deep, practical knowledge of the field services industry is essential, gained through direct experience working within or alongside offshore, onshore, or project-based workforce environments. Candidates must bring firsthand understanding of how these workforces are structured, deployed, and managed.

  • Demonstrated understanding of different offshore employment models and able to advise customers on how to design compliant, commercially sound workforce arrangements across multiple jurisdictions.

  • Mobility or Commercial or Tax exposure is an added bonus.

  • Willingness to travel inside and outside of territory (~10% or less).

  • Outstanding presentation and storytelling skills, always focusing on customer value (multilingual capability is a bonus).

  • Entrepreneurial DNA with a positive, can-do attitude.

  • A collaborative, team player is essential.

  • Comfortable in a start-up, fast-moving environment.

Senior Product Manager - Equity

Summary

As a Senior Product Manager, you will own the execution and evolution of Deel’s Equity product, a fast-growing and strategically important area within our Core Data organisation. You’ll work closely with senior leaders, engineers, and cross-functional partners across compliance, payroll, and compensation to deliver a best-in-class global equity experience.

This role sits at the intersection of fintech, payroll, HR, and compliance. You will be responsible for translating complex equity concepts into scalable product solutions, helping power one of Deel’s most important and rapidly expanding revenue lines.

Why this role

Equity is a critical component of how companies attract, retain, and reward talent globally, yet it remains highly complex, fragmented, and manual — especially when you go global.

At Deel, we are building a global equity platform that connects equity data, payroll, and compliance into a seamless experience. Today, we’ve only scratched the surface, with significant opportunities to automate manual workflows, expand into new worker types, and deeply integrate equity into broader products like compensation and total rewards.

You’ll join at a pivotal moment: owning execution for a high-growth product with low market penetration, strong demand, and a clear path to scale.

What you’ll do

  • Own the day-to-day execution of the Equity product, working across both equity data and equity services

  • Drive delivery of a roadmap focused on AI, automation, scalability, and global compliance

  • Partner closely with engineering (distributed across EMEA and APAC) to ship high-impact features quickly

  • Work cross-functionally with compliance, payroll, and compensation product teams to ensure seamless integration

  • Define how Deel ingests and models equity data from external systems (e.g., stock administration platforms like Carta, Shareworks, Fidelity, and more)

  • Build and improve workflows around key equity events (e.g. vesting, reporting, compliance handling)

  • Identify and prioritise opportunities to reduce manual processes and operational overhead

  • Support expansion of the equity product into new geographies and additional worker types

  • Contribute to connecting equity into Deel’s broader ecosystem (e.g. total compensation and rewards)

  • Communicate progress, priorities, and trade-offs clearly to stakeholders across product and business teams

  • Operate in a fast-paced, high-ownership environment where speed and execution are critical

What success looks like in 12 months

  • Delivery of a clear and well-executed roadmap focused on AI, automation, and product expansion

  • Reduction in manual operational processes through scalable product improvements

  • Increased adoption of the equity product across new worker types and geographies

  • Strong collaboration with compliance, payroll, and compensation product teams

  • Measurable growth in product usage and contribution to revenue within the Equity service

What you’ll bring

  • 5 years of experience in Product Management

  • Strong execution skills with the ability to deliver quickly in a fast-moving environment

  • Experience working in fintech, payroll, compliance, or other complex regulated domains (highly preferred)

  • Solid understanding of financial concepts, or the ability to ramp quickly on equity and compensation structures

  • Experience working cross-functionally with engineering and non-technical stakeholders

  • Comfortable operating in ambiguity and owning a product area end-to-end

  • Strong communication skills with the ability to simplify complex topics

  • Experience working in distributed, global teams

  • Candidate must be based in EMEA

Nice to have

  • Experience with equity management, stock administration platforms, or cap table systems

  • Background in payroll or compliance-heavy environments

  • Experience building products that integrate with external financial or HR systems

About the Role

We're hiring a hands-on Design Leader to join our Connectivity team at Deel. The Connectivity team sits at the heart of Deel's platform infrastructure — enabling partnerships, integrations, developer experience, and operational tooling that powers Deel's evolution from a standalone product into a true platform.

You'll own design across highly technical and strategic surfaces: API and developer ecosystems, the app store, white-label solutions, partner portals, and AI-powered customer experiences like Deel Inbox. These products serve a diverse set of stakeholders — from enterprise clients to startup developers to internal ops teams — and require someone who can bring both system-level thinking and sharp execution to the work.

We're looking for someone who gets deep into product work, has a strong doer mindset, thinks big, and moves fast. This is a different kind of leadership. Not decks, not endless strategy debates, not big-org politics. It's about leading by doing. Setting the bar. Shipping strong work. Raising others through example.

It's a hybrid role: you'll lead a small team and still design yourself.

What You'll Do

  • ~30% Vision. Partnering with PDE Directors to shape the long-term direction of Deel's Connectivity platform. Connecting dots across journeys. Challenging existing paradigms. Creating a unified, coherent experience across API docs, integration flows, partner-facing tools, and AI-powered surfaces.

  • ~40% Operator mode. Working directly with designers on active platform, B2B, and developer-facing projects. Tightening UX. Solving complexity. Raising quality in real time. Translating technical constraints into intuitive, self-serve user flows.

  • ~30% Team leadership. Managing and developing a small team through hands-on collaboration. Creating clarity. Raising the bar. Putting them first. Coaching and mentoring designers to elevate craft and invest more consistently in research and data.

What We're Looking For

  • 8+ years in Product Design, 3+ years operating at a very senior IC level (Lead / Staff)

  • No less than 1.5 years of experience managing designers (up to 5 direct reports)

  • Experience at scale-ups, tech companies or developer platform companies

  • Experience with API/developer products, integrations, or platform ecosystems — this is a strong plus and central to this role

  • Strong craft across interaction and visual design; comfortable across B2B, developer, and enterprise surfaces

  • Experience designing for documentation, onboarding flows, and developer-facing UX

  • Comfortable with complex system architecture and technical constraints

  • AI: Curiosity about AI and emerging technologies; experience designing AI-assisted or AI-powered product experiences, or using AI tools as part of your design workflow. A portfolio that includes examples of work related to AI-powered features, AI-assisted workflows, or experiments with AI tools.

  • Experienced in multi-sided platform design (developers, partners, end-users)

  • Data-savvy; comfortable measuring success through adoption and integration metrics

  • Solid understanding of both enterprise and startup needs

You Should Be Someone Who

  • Thrives in velocity and doesn't wait for perfect clarity

  • Makes good decisions under pressure

  • Puts the team first and holds a high bar for yourself and others

  • Leads by example through strong IC contribution

  • Can facilitate execution while raising — not lowering — the quality bar

  • Cares about details and outcomes

  • Integrates research into product decisions naturally

Design & AI at Deel

AI is changing how products are designed and built. At Deel, we're excited about designers who are exploring how AI can enhance both the design process and the product experience. We're looking for designers who are curious about emerging technologies and who actively experiment with AI in their work — whether that's improving design workflows, prototyping faster, exploring new interaction patterns, or helping shape AI-powered product experiences. If you've worked on AI-related features or used AI tools as part of your design process, we'd love to see examples of that work in your portfolio.

As the fastest-growing Software as a Service (SaaS) company in history, Deel is transforming how global talent connects with world-class companies – breaking down borders that have traditionally limited both hiring and career opportunities. We're not just building software; we're creating the infrastructure for the future of work, enabling a more diverse and inclusive global economy. In 2024 alone, we paid $11.2 billion to workers in nearly 100 currencies and provided healthcare and benefits to workers in 109 countries—ensuring people get paid and protected, no matter where they are.

Our momentum is reflected in our achievements and customer satisfaction: CNBC Disruptor 50,  Forbes Cloud 100, Deloitte Fast 500, and repeated recognition on Y Combinator’s top companies list – all while maintaining a 4.83 average rating from 15,000 reviews across G2, Trustpilot, Captera, Apple and Google.

Your experience at Deel will be a career accelerator. At the forefront of the global work revolution, you'll tackle complex challenges that impact millions of people's working lives. With our momentum—backed by a $17.3 billion valuation and $1 B in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in just over five years—you'll drive meaningful impact while building expertise that makes you a sought-after leader in the transformation of global work.

What you'll do at Deel

  • Help shape and implement the go-to-market strategy in your region of focus, drive annual and quarterly planning and target setting, conduct independent analysis of the business, gather insights, make compelling recommendations to GTM and GTMOps leadership, and drive change management to improve the business in line with Sales Lead priorities.

  • Own territory carving, territory updates, quota-setting analysis, and other data-driven priorities in planning and execution of the business.

  • Help lead Forecasting and business inspection cadence.

  • Spearhead complex change management initiatives that improve the performance of the business.

  • Act as regional representative with other Sales Ops functions: sales compensation, pricing, and deal desk.

  • Build analytical dashboards in Google Sheets or Looker to drive strategic business decisions.

  • Work in a highly collaborative manner to drive strategy, revenue growth and scalable operations with various stakeholders across our Go-To-Market functions (e.g. Sales, Account Management, Sales Development, Customer Success, Growth, Marketing), GTMOps teams (Sales Ops, Systems & Process, Pipeline Operations, Enablement), and occasionally our Product team.

Some key qualifications

  • 5+ years of demonstrated success in sales operations, revenue operations, or a business operations function at leading tech companies

  • Fluency with Salesforce and adjacent sales productivity applications

  • Strong understanding of general SaaS operations, best practices, and industry standards

  • Experience working on a global team and collaborating with global stakeholders

  • Excellent communication skills with the ability to influence rapid business outcomes

  • Strong problem solver with the ability to drive detailed cross-functional projects

  • Nimble, adaptable, resilient, resourceful and gritty. Good sense of humor is a plus!

Ce que nous faisons définit qui nous sommes.

Deel est la plateforme tout-en-un de paie et de RH dédiée aux équipes internationales. Notre vision est de donner à chaque individu, équipe et entreprise accès aux opportunités mondiales. Conçue pour répondre aux modes de travail actuels, Deel regroupe le SIRH, la paie, la conformité légale, les avantages sociaux, la performance et la gestion des équipements au sein d'une interface unique et fluide. Grâce à des outils propulsés par l'IA et une infrastructure de paie interne, Deel accompagne tous les types de travailleurs dans plus de 150 pays, aidant ainsi les entreprises à se développer de manière plus intelligente, plus rapide et plus conforme.

Comptant parmi les plus grandes entreprises au monde, notre équipe de 7 000 collaborateurs s'étend sur plus de 100 pays, parle 74 langues et porte une culture connectée et dynamique qui favorise l'apprentissage continu et l'innovation pour nos clients.

Pourquoi devriez-vous prendre part à notre succès ?

En tant qu'entreprise de Logiciel en tant que Service (SaaS) à la croissance la plus rapide de l'histoire, Deel transforme la manière dont les talents mondiaux se connectent aux entreprises de premier plan ; brisant les frontières qui limitent traditionnellement l'embauche et les opportunités de carrière. Nous ne nous contentons pas de créer un logiciel ; nous bâtissons l'infrastructure du futur du travail, favorisant une économie mondiale plus diverse et inclusive. En 2024 seulement, nous avons versé 11,2 milliards de dollars à des travailleurs dans près de 100 devises et fournit une couverture santé et des avantages sociaux dans 109 pays, garantissant que chacun soit rémunéré et protégé, peu importe où il se trouve.

Notre élan se reflète dans nos réussites et la satisfaction de nos clients : CNBC Disruptor 50, Forbes Cloud 100, Deloitte Fast 500, ainsi qu'une reconnaissance répétée dans la liste des meilleures entreprises de Y Combinator, tout en maintenant une note moyenne de 4,83 basée sur 15 000 avis via G2, Trustpilot, Capterra, Apple et Google.

Votre expérience chez Deel sera un véritable accélérateur de carrière. À l'avant-garde de la révolution mondiale du travail, vous relèverez des défis complexes qui impactent la vie professionnelle de millions de personnes. Porté par notre dynamique, soutenue par une valorisation de 17,3 milliards de dollars et un revenu annuel récurrent (ARR) d'un milliard de dollars en un peu plus de cinq ans, vous générerez un impact significatif tout en développant une expertise qui fera de vous un leader convoité de la transformation du travail à l'échelle mondiale.

CE QUE VOUS FEREZ CHEZ DEEL

  • Aider à façonner et à mettre en œuvre la stratégie d'accès au marché dans votre région, diriger la planification annuelle et trimestrielle ainsi que la définition des objectifs. Mener une analyse, recueillir des informations, faire des recommandations convaincantes à la direction GTM et GTMOps, et diriger la gestion du changement pour améliorer l'entreprise en fonction des priorités du directeur commercial.

  • Être responsable de la connaissance du territoire, de l'analyse de la définition des quotas et d'autres priorités basées sur les données dans la planification et l'exécution des opérations

  • Aider à diriger la prévision et l’inspection des équipes de ventes

  • Diriger des initiatives complexes de gestion du changement afin d’améliorer la performance des équipes de ventes

  • Agir en tant que représentant régional auprès des autres fonctions des opérations commerciales : rémunération des ventes, tarification.

  • Créer des tableaux de bord dans Google Sheets ou Looker pour guider les décisions commerciales stratégiques.

  • Collaborer avec les équipes pour guider la stratégie, la croissance des revenus et des opérations avec les diverses parties prenantes dans nos fonctions d'accès au marché (par exemple, Ventes, Gestion de compte, Développement commercial, Succès client, Croissance, Marketing), les équipes GTMOps (Opérations commerciales, Systèmes et processus, Opérations de pipeline, Habilitation) et occasionnellement notre équipe produit.

CERTAINES QUALIFICATIONS CLÉS

  • 5+ ans de succès démontré dans les opérations commerciales, les opérations de revenus ou une fonction d'opérations commerciales dans l’une des principales entreprises technologiques

  • Maîtrise de Salesforce et des applications complémentaires de productivité commerciale

  • Solide compréhension des opérations SaaS générales, des bonnes pratiques et des normes de l'industrie

  • Expérience de travail dans une équipe globale

  • Excellentes compétences en communication attendues

  • Capacité à résoudre les problèmes ,de conduire des projets interfonctionnels détaillés

  • Agile, adaptable, résilient, ingénieux et tenace. Un bon sens de l'humour est un atout!

Rémunération Globale

Nos collaborateurs méritent une rémunération juste et compétitive qui s'adapte à leur réalité. Avec des avantages sociaux et des gratifications évolutives, notre programme de rémunération globale reflète notre engagement en faveur de l'inclusion et de l'accessibilité pour tous.

Ce dont vous bénéficierez :

  • Des opportunités d'attribution d'actions (stock options) selon votre rôle, votre statut d'emploi et votre localisation.

  • Des avantages et privilèges supplémentaires basés sur votre statut d'emploi et votre pays.

  • La flexibilité du travail à distance, incluant un accès optionnel aux espaces WeWork.

Égalité des chances et Diversité

Chez Deel, nous sommes un employeur garantissant l'égalité des chances. Nous valorisons la diversité et encourageons vivement les candidatures de personnes qualifiées, sans distinction de race, de religion, de sexe, d'origine nationale, d'identité de genre, d'orientation sexuelle, d'âge, d'état civil, de statut de vétéran, de handicap, de grossesse ou de maternité, ou de toute autre caractéristique protégée par la loi.

Sauf accord contraire, nous communiquons avec les candidats via des adresses e-mail spécifiques à Deel (se terminant par @deel.com ou celles de sociétés acquises comme @payspace.com et @paygroup.com). Vous pouvez consulter nos offres d'emploi à jour sur notre page carrières.

Accessibilité et Processus de Recrutement

Deel s'engage à offrir des aménagements sur demande tout au long du processus de recrutement, de sélection et d'évaluation pour les candidats en situation de handicap. Si vous avez besoin d'un aménagement, veuillez en informer notre équipe d'acquisition de talents via [ce lien] ; un membre de l'équipe vous contactera pour garantir votre participation équitable. En cas de difficulté d'accès au formulaire, contactez-nous à recruiting@deel.com.

Notre processus repose principalement sur des entretiens et des évaluations liées au poste. Dans certains cas restreints, nous pouvons examiner des informations contextuelles informelles pertinentes pour le rôle, conformément à nos obligations de confidentialité et d'équité.



Utilisation de l'IA et de la Technologie

Ce processus de candidature utilise des outils d'aide à la décision automatisée (AEDT) et des systèmes d'IA pour évaluer les candidats en fonction de leur niveau d'expérience, de leurs compétences techniques et de leurs qualifications. En tant qu'entreprise 100 % en télétravail, nous utilisons également des technologies de détection de fraude et de deepfake propulsées par l'IA pour vérifier l'authenticité de l'identité des candidats lors des évaluations. Ce traitement est effectué dans le respect des lois sur la protection des données, la gouvernance de l'IA et le droit du travail. Nous garantissons qu'une supervision humaine est maintenue pour toutes les décisions finales d'embauche. Vos données personnelles ne sont pas utilisées pour entraîner des modèles d'IA. Pour plus d'informations, consultez notre Politique de Confidentialité.

Summary


The Sales Development Representative (SDR) at Deel is more than a single role—it’s the engine behind our global growth. We are constantly looking for ambitious builders to join our specialized SDR tracks, including Deel IT, Mobility (Immigration), Core (HR/Payroll), and GWC (Customer Expansion).

By applying here, you are entering our general SDR talent pool, where we match candidates to the team that best fits their strengths and interests. If you have a specific interest in one of our segments, please let us know in your application—we’re happy to tailor your interview path to the team that fits you best.

As our Sales Development Representative (SDR), you’ll play a vital role in our team by opening doors to new and existing opportunities and building strong relationships across all levels of our target customer segments. You’ll contribute directly to our growth, creating a significant impact in the company’s journey.

In this role, you’ll collaborate closely with our sales team, conduct market research to identify potential customers, help expand current business, and qualify leads through insightful conversations about global HR and workforce solutions.

Please note: This role requires that you reside within the United States or Canada.
Key Responsibilities

  • Drive Growth: Fuel our company’s growth by generating high-quality outbound leads (both new business and existing business) that can convert into meaningful sales opportunities and business wins.

  • Collaborate & Learn: Work alongside a seasoned sales team to build out our world-class Sales Playbook and Tech Stack, while sharing your insights to make these resources even better.

  • Engage & Educate: Engage leads through various channels—phone, email, events, webinars, and campaigns—to educate them and build interest in our solutions.

  • Master the Process: Develop a deep understanding of each stage in the lead generation process, from marketing initiatives and touchpoints to the transition of leads to an Account Executive.

  • Build & Refine Outreach: Create targeted email sequences and confidently reach out via cold calls to identify key contacts, establish relationships, and deepen connections.

  • Follow Through: Conduct timely follow-ups to nurture relationships and set up valuable meetings with our Account Executives.

What We’re Looking For

  • Growth-Oriented: You have a proactive approach and thrive in a fast-paced environment with a strong growth mindset.

  • Strong Communicator: You’re skilled at crafting messages that resonate and have experience writing emails and campaign copy that converts.

  • Team Player: You’re a natural relationship-builder who collaborates well with both internal and external teams, bringing fresh ideas to improve processes and communication.

  • Adaptable & Curious: You enjoy learning, embrace change, and are eager to evolve in a dynamic work environment.

Bonus Points (Not Required)

  • Experience in HR, Fintech, IT or related fields

  • Background in a fast-growing startup

  • Previous experience in a remote or distributed team

If this sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you! These qualities are essential for success in this role, so please apply if you’re excited to bring these skills to our team and grow with us. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds and are looking for passionate individuals ready to make an impact.

Want to stand out? We encourage you to prospect us! Reach out to the hiring team to let us know you’ve applied—show us your skills in action and demonstrate your proactive approach to building connections.


Ce que nous faisons définit qui nous sommes.

Deel est la plateforme tout-en-un de paie et de RH dédiée aux équipes internationales. Notre vision est de donner à chaque individu, équipe et entreprise accès aux opportunités mondiales. Conçue pour répondre aux modes de travail actuels, Deel regroupe le SIRH, la paie, la conformité légale, les avantages sociaux, la performance et la gestion des équipements au sein d'une interface unique et fluide. Grâce à des outils propulsés par l'IA et une infrastructure de paie interne, Deel accompagne tous les types de travailleurs dans plus de 150 pays, aidant ainsi les entreprises à se développer de manière plus intelligente, plus rapide et plus conforme.

Comptant parmi les plus grandes entreprises au monde, notre équipe de 7 000 collaborateurs s'étend sur plus de 100 pays, parle 74 langues et porte une culture connectée et dynamique qui favorise l'apprentissage continu et l'innovation pour nos clients.

Pourquoi devriez-vous prendre part à notre succès ?

En tant qu'entreprise de Logiciel en tant que Service (SaaS) à la croissance la plus rapide de l'histoire, Deel transforme la manière dont les talents mondiaux se connectent aux entreprises de premier plan ; brisant les frontières qui limitent traditionnellement l'embauche et les opportunités de carrière. Nous ne nous contentons pas de créer un logiciel ; nous bâtissons l'infrastructure du futur du travail, favorisant une économie mondiale plus diverse et inclusive. En 2024 seulement, nous avons versé 11,2 milliards de dollars à des travailleurs dans près de 100 devises et fournit une couverture santé et des avantages sociaux dans 109 pays, garantissant que chacun soit rémunéré et protégé, peu importe où il se trouve.

Notre élan se reflète dans nos réussites et la satisfaction de nos clients : CNBC Disruptor 50, Forbes Cloud 100, Deloitte Fast 500, ainsi qu'une reconnaissance répétée dans la liste des meilleures entreprises de Y Combinator, tout en maintenant une note moyenne de 4,83 basée sur 15 000 avis via G2, Trustpilot, Capterra, Apple et Google.

Votre expérience chez Deel sera un véritable accélérateur de carrière. À l'avant-garde de la révolution mondiale du travail, vous relèverez des défis complexes qui impactent la vie professionnelle de millions de personnes. Porté par notre dynamique, soutenue par une valorisation de 17,3 milliards de dollars et un revenu annuel récurrent (ARR) d'un milliard de dollars en un peu plus de cinq ans, vous générerez un impact significatif tout en développant une expertise qui fera de vous un leader convoité de la transformation du travail à l'échelle mondiale.


Résumé

Le poste de Représentant(e) en Développement des Ventes (SDR) chez Deel est bien plus qu'un simple rôle, c'est le moteur de notre croissance mondiale. Nous recherchons en permanence des bâtisseurs ambitieux pour rejoindre nos équipes SDR spécialisées, notamment Deel IT, Mobilité (Immigration), Core (RH/Paie) et GWC (Expansion Client).

En postulant ici, vous intégrez notre vivier général de talents SDR, où nous associons les candidats à l'équipe qui correspond le mieux à leurs points forts et à leurs intérêts. Si vous avez une préférence pour l'un de nos segments, n'hésitez pas à nous en informer dans votre candidature, nous serons heureux d'adapter votre parcours d'entretien à l'équipe qui vous convient le mieux.

En tant que Représentant(e) en Développement des Ventes (SDR), vous jouerez un rôle essentiel au sein de notre équipe en ouvrant de nouvelles opportunités et en établissant des relations solides à tous les niveaux de nos segments clients cibles. Vous contribuerez directement à notre croissance, ayant un impact significatif sur le parcours de l'entreprise.

Dans ce rôle, vous collaborerez étroitement avec notre équipe commerciale, mènerez des études de marché pour identifier des clients potentiels, aiderez à développer les affaires existantes et qualifierez les prospects grâce à des échanges constructifs sur les solutions mondiales en matière de RH et de gestion des effectifs.

À noter : Ce poste nécessite que vous résidiez aux États-Unis ou au Canada.

Responsabilités Principales

  • Stimuler la croissance : Alimenter la croissance de l'entreprise en générant des leads sortants de haute qualité (nouvelles affaires et affaires existantes) susceptibles de se convertir en opportunités commerciales significatives.

  • Collaborer & Apprendre : Travailler aux côtés d'une équipe commerciale expérimentée pour développer notre Sales Playbook et notre stack technologique de classe mondiale, tout en partageant vos connaissances pour améliorer ces ressources.

  • Engager & Éduquer : Interagir avec les prospects via différents canaux, téléphone, e-mail, événements, webinaires et campagnes, pour les informer et susciter leur intérêt pour nos solutions.

  • Maîtriser le processus : Développer une compréhension approfondie de chaque étape du processus de génération de leads, des initiatives marketing aux points de contact, jusqu'au transfert des leads à un Account Executive.

  • Construire & Affiner les approches : Créer des séquences d'e-mails ciblées et effectuer des appels à froid en toute confiance pour identifier les contacts clés, établir des relations et renforcer les liens.

  • Assurer le suivi : Effectuer des relances en temps opportun pour entretenir les relations et organiser des réunions de valeur avec nos Account Executives.

Profil Recherché

  • Orienté(e) croissance : Vous avez une approche proactive et vous épanouissez dans un environnement dynamique avec un fort état d'esprit de développement.

  • Excellent(e) communicant(e) : Vous savez rédiger des messages percutants et avez de l'expérience dans la rédaction d'e-mails et de contenus de campagne.

  • Esprit d'équipe : Vous êtes naturellement à l'aise pour créer des relations et collaborer avec des équipes internes et externes, apportant de nouvelles idées pour améliorer les processus et la communication.

  • Adaptable & Curieux(se) : Vous aimez apprendre, vous vous adaptez facilement au changement et êtes impatient(e) d'évoluer dans un environnement de travail dynamique.

Atouts Supplémentaires (Non Obligatoires)

  • Expérience dans les RH, la Fintech, l'IT ou des domaines connexes

  • Expérience dans une start-up en forte croissance

  • Expérience préalable au sein d'une équipe à distance ou distribuée

Growth Solutions Architect (GTM AI Operator)| UKI

You'll build, run, and continuously improve the AI-powered systems that drive Deel's global go-to-market engine. This is a hands-on, builder-first role — you're not advising on what to automate, you're doing it. As part of a centralized AI operations team, you'll deploy autonomous agents, re-engineer revenue workflows, and push the limits of what's possible across sales, SDR, and marketing motions. The work you ship will directly accelerate pipeline and improve how our revenue teams operate every day.

 

What you'll do at Deel

Build and deploy AI agents Prototype and ship autonomous AI agents for critical GTM workflows — inbound qualification, outbound engagement, and the highest-ROI automation opportunities — using Claude Code, n8n, AI Studio, and GitHub.

Operate and improve the GTM automation stack Run, monitor, and iterate on live workflows. You don't set it and walk away — you own the quality and performance of what's in production.

Drive AI tool adoption Manage the end-to-end rollout of new AI tools, including building training materials and supporting change management to ensure strong adoption across revenue teams.

Support revenue tech stack performance Contribute to the health and integration of Deel's revenue tech ecosystem (Salesforce, Outreach, LISN) — flagging performance gaps and working with stakeholders to resolve them.

Collaborate with GTM teams Partner with Sales, SDR, and Marketing to understand workflow pain points, translate them into automation opportunities, and deliver solutions that measurably improve productivity and conversion.

Build scalable, documented processes Design and document workflows, SOPs, and playbooks so the systems you build can scale across a global org without depending on any single person.

Contribute to pipeline analysis Support funnel diagnostics by analyzing performance data, surfacing blockers, and helping the team close gaps between pipeline targets and actuals.

 
 

Key qualifications

  • 2–4 years of experience in RevOps, GTM Ops, Growth, or Sales Operations — with hands-on experience shipping AI or LLM-powered workflows in production. This is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have.

  • Proven ability to build agentic workflows using modern AI tooling (Claude Code, n8n, AI Studio, or equivalents). You've moved beyond prompting into actual deployment.

  • Strong working knowledge of CRM and sales engagement tools — you know how Salesforce flows, lead routing, and engagement sequences work well enough to troubleshoot and build on top of them.

  • Solid analytical skills. You're comfortable in spreadsheets and can work with data to tell a clear story about what's working and what isn't. SQL is a plus.

  • Deep familiarity with demand funnel metrics — MQL, SAL, SQL, closed-won — and how they connect across segments.

  • You communicate clearly and proactively. You surface problems early and come with proposed solutions, not just observations.

  • Proven ability to manage projects end-to-end — scoping, execution, stakeholder alignment, and follow-through.

  • Comfortable with ambiguity. The tools, scope, and methods in this space change fast. You adapt and keep moving.

 
 

Why join us?

This role exists at the intersection of two things Deel takes seriously: being the most advanced AI-powered people platform in the world, and running a revenue engine that operates across 150+ countries. You'll build on real infrastructure at real scale — the agents and workflows you deploy will run across one of the fastest-growing GTM orgs in SaaS. You won't be piloting ideas. You'll be shipping them.

Build scalable, documented processes Design and document workflows, SOPs, and playbooks so the systems you build can scale across a global org without depending on any single person.

Contribute to pipeline analysis Support funnel diagnostics by analyzing performance data, surfacing blockers, and helping the team close gaps between pipeline targets and actuals.

 
 

Key qualifications

  • 2–4 years of experience in RevOps, GTM Ops, Growth, or Sales Operations — with hands-on experience shipping AI or LLM-powered workflows in production. This is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have.

  • Proven ability to build agentic workflows using modern AI tooling (Claude Code, n8n, AI Studio, or equivalents). You've moved beyond prompting into actual deployment.

  • Strong working knowledge of CRM and sales engagement tools — you know how Salesforce flows, lead routing, and engagement sequences work well enough to troubleshoot and build on top of them.

  • Solid analytical skills. You're comfortable in spreadsheets and can work with data to tell a clear story about what's working and what isn't. SQL is a plus.

  • Deep familiarity with demand funnel metrics — MQL, SAL, SQL, closed-won — and how they connect across segments.

  • You communicate clearly and proactively. You surface problems early and come with proposed solutions, not just observations.

  • Proven ability to manage projects end-to-end — scoping, execution, stakeholder alignment, and follow-through.

  • Comfortable with ambiguity. The tools, scope, and methods in this space change fast. You adapt and keep moving.

 
 

Why join us?

This role exists at the intersection of two things Deel takes seriously: being the most advanced AI-powered people platform in the world, and running a revenue engine that operates across 150+ countries. You'll build on real infrastructure at real scale — the agents and workflows you deploy will run across one of the fastest-growing GTM orgs in SaaS. You won't be piloting ideas. Summary:

Deel IT exists to equip the world's talent with the means to do their best work. Through one slick platform customers can procure, deploy, repair & recover entire fleets of devices and home office equipment for their employees in over 120 countries.

Your Responsibilities:

  • Hungry for success: You’re passionate and driven, and you’re willing to take risks, be creative, and always resilient.

  • Seasoned sales professional: You have experience selling to SMB (Up to 200 employees) sized customers, ideally a software-led product, and having strong success as a net new logo hunter. 

  • Ownership and execution: You take ownership of the complete sales cycle from start to finish, navigating complex commercial deals by building strong relationships and negotiating to a close.

  • Strong Communicator: You are an excellent communicator with great presentation skills. You can effectively articulate and leverage the Deel IT value proposition to win the trust of customers and close deals.

  • Owner mindset: You're passionate about Deel IT's product and mission and positively display this at every interaction within the sales cycle and beyond.

Key Requirements:

  • Earned your stripes: You have 2+ years of sales quota carrying experience - ideally in IT SaaS (MDM, IAM, device lifecycle, SaaS procurement, or adjacent categories.

  • Seen great success: You have been an integral part of at least one great business success story, and know what it takes to deliver outlier results.

  • Closing: A strong background in getting B2B software-led product sales across the line. You are an expert individual contributor with an excellent track record.

  • Builder: You're willing and able to build out strong pipelines, whilst fluidly partnering with a BDR.

  • Accurate and accountable: You can accurately forecast your sales results, you see the gaps in your pipeline and you are ahead of the curve, you strive to always go above and beyond.

  • Teamwork: You have experience collaborating with teams across a growing company and building strong relationships.

  • Thrive in a global remote team environment.

Ce que nous faisons définit qui nous sommes.

Deel est la plateforme tout-en-un de paie et de RH dédiée aux équipes internationales. Notre vision est de donner à chaque individu, équipe et entreprise accès aux opportunités mondiales. Conçue pour répondre aux modes de travail actuels, Deel regroupe le SIRH, la paie, la conformité légale, les avantages sociaux, la performance et la gestion des équipements au sein d'une interface unique et fluide. Grâce à des outils propulsés par l'IA et une infrastructure de paie interne, Deel accompagne tous les types de travailleurs dans plus de 150 pays, aidant ainsi les entreprises à se développer de manière plus intelligente, plus rapide et plus conforme.

Comptant parmi les plus grandes entreprises au monde, notre équipe de 7 000 collaborateurs s'étend sur plus de 100 pays, parle 74 langues et porte une culture connectée et dynamique qui favorise l'apprentissage continu et l'innovation pour nos clients.

Pourquoi devriez-vous prendre part à notre succès ?

En tant qu'entreprise de Logiciel en tant que Service (SaaS) à la croissance la plus rapide de l'histoire, Deel transforme la manière dont les talents mondiaux se connectent aux entreprises de premier plan ; brisant les frontières qui limitent traditionnellement l'embauche et les opportunités de carrière. Nous ne nous contentons pas de créer un logiciel ; nous bâtissons l'infrastructure du futur du travail, favorisant une économie mondiale plus diverse et inclusive. En 2024 seulement, nous avons versé 11,2 milliards de dollars à des travailleurs dans près de 100 devises et fournit une couverture santé et des avantages sociaux dans 109 pays, garantissant que chacun soit rémunéré et protégé, peu importe où il se trouve.

Notre élan se reflète dans nos réussites et la satisfaction de nos clients : CNBC Disruptor 50, Forbes Cloud 100, Deloitte Fast 500, ainsi qu'une reconnaissance répétée dans la liste des meilleures entreprises de Y Combinator, tout en maintenant une note moyenne de 4,83 basée sur 15 000 avis via G2, Trustpilot, Capterra, Apple et Google.

Votre expérience chez Deel sera un véritable accélérateur de carrière. À l'avant-garde de la révolution mondiale du travail, vous relèverez des défis complexes qui impactent la vie professionnelle de millions de personnes. Porté par notre dynamique, soutenue par une valorisation de 17,3 milliards de dollars et un revenu annuel récurrent (ARR) d'un milliard de dollars en un peu plus de cinq ans, vous générerez un impact significatif tout en développant une expertise qui fera de vous un leader convoité de la transformation du travail à l'échelle mondiale.

Résumé : Deel IT existe pour donner aux talents du monde entier les moyens de faire leur meilleur travail. Grâce à une plateforme intuitive, les clients peuvent se procurer, déployer, réparer et récupérer des parcs entiers d'appareils et d'équipements de bureau à domicile pour leurs employés dans plus de 120 pays.

Vos responsabilités :

  • Soif de réussite : Vous êtes passionné(e) et déterminé(e), prêt(e) à prendre des risques, à faire preuve de créativité et à faire preuve de résilience en toutes circonstances.

  • Professionnel(le) de la vente aguerri(e) : Vous avez de l'expérience dans la vente auprès de clients de taille PME (jusqu'à 200 employés), idéalement avec un produit axé sur les logiciels, et vous avez obtenu d'excellents résultats en tant que chasseur(se) de nouveaux comptes.

  • Prise en charge et exécution : Vous prenez en charge l'ensemble du cycle de vente du début à la fin, en naviguant dans des ententes commerciales complexes grâce à des relations solides et à des négociations menées jusqu'à la conclusion.

  • Excellent(e) communicateur(trice) : Vous communiquez avec aisance et possédez de solides compétences en présentation. Vous savez articuler et mettre en valeur la proposition de valeur de Deel IT pour gagner la confiance des clients et conclure des ententes.

  • Esprit de propriétaire : Vous êtes passionné(e) par le produit et la mission de Deel IT, et vous le démontrez positivement à chaque interaction, que ce soit dans le cadre du cycle de vente ou au-delà.

Exigences principales :

  • Expérience éprouvée : Vous avez plus de 2 ans d'expérience en vente avec quota — idéalement dans le domaine des TI SaaS (MDM, IAM, cycle de vie des appareils, approvisionnement SaaS ou catégories connexes).

  • Bilan de succès : Vous avez joué un rôle clé dans au moins une belle réussite commerciale et savez ce qu'il faut pour livrer des résultats exceptionnels.

  • Conclusion de ventes : Solide expérience dans la conclusion de ventes de produits logiciels B2B. Vous êtes un(e) contributeur(trice) individuel(le) expert(e) avec un excellent bilan.

  • Bâtisseur(se) : Vous êtes en mesure de développer des pipelines solides tout en collaborant de façon fluide avec un(e) représentant(e) au développement des affaires (BDR).

  • Rigueur et responsabilité : Vous pouvez établir des prévisions de ventes précises, vous identifiez les lacunes dans votre pipeline et vous gardez une longueur d'avance, en vous efforçant toujours de dépasser les attentes.

  • Esprit d'équipe : Vous avez de l'expérience en collaboration avec des équipes au sein d'une entreprise en croissance et dans l'établissement de relations solides.

  • À l'aise dans un environnement d'équipe mondiale en télétravail.

Si ce poste vous correspond, nous serions ravis de vous entendre ! Ces qualités sont essentielles pour réussir dans ce rôle, alors postulez si vous êtes enthousiaste à l'idée d'apporter ces compétences à notre équipe et de grandir avec nous. Nous accueillons des candidats de tous horizons et recherchons des personnes passionnées, prêtes à faire la différence.

Envie de vous démarquer ? Nous vous encourageons à nous prospecter ! Contactez l'équipe de recrutement pour nous informer de votre candidature, montrez-nous vos compétences en action et démontrez votre approche proactive dans la construction de relations.

Nos collaborateurs méritent une rémunération juste et compétitive qui s'adapte à leur réalité. Avec des avantages sociaux et des gratifications évolutives, notre programme de rémunération globale reflète notre engagement en faveur de l'inclusion et de l'accessibilité pour tous.

Summary

The Manager, Sales Development Representative is responsible for leading a high-performing team of SDRs focused on generating qualified pipeline and supporting revenue growth across a defined segment or product. This role oversees the design and execution of prospecting strategies, ensures consistent lead quality, and drives operational excellence across outbound and inbound motions. It also plays a key role in developing team capabilities, optimizing workflows, and ensuring a strong handoff to downstream commercial teams.

Responsibilities

  • Hire, onboard, and develop a high-performing team of SDRs, fostering a culture of accountability, continuous learning, and high engagement.

  • Set clear goals and performance expectations aligned to pipeline generation, conversion metrics, and meeting targets.

  • Build and implement scalable outbound and/or inbound sales development playbooks, cadences, processes, and best practices.

  • Drive consistent execution of prospecting activities, ensuring adherence to quality standards and effective communication with potential customers.

  • Provide ongoing coaching, feedback, and skill development through regular 1:1s, role plays, call reviews, and team training sessions.

  • Identify process improvements to increase SDR efficiency, productivity, and operational scalability.

  • Motivate the team through recognition, incentives, and transparent communication of expectations and results.

  • Maintain accurate reporting of SDR activity, pipeline creation, and team performance in CRM and engagement tools.

  • Support career path development and succession planning for SDRs.

Qualifications

  • 3+ years of experience in sales or sales leadership, ideally within SDR, BDR, or pipeline-focused functions.

  • Proven success leading or scaling SDR teams in fast-paced, high-growth environments.

  • Strong ability to design and implement outbound and/or inbound sales development playbooks, cadences, and prospecting frameworks.

  • Demonstrated experience using data and analytics to drive decisions, improve performance, and identify areas of opportunity.

  • Excellent coaching, mentoring, and talent development skills.

  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills, able to influence across teams and collaborate effectively.

  • Highly organized, proactive, and able to operate independently while managing multiple priorities.

  • Experience with CRM and sales engagement tools (e.g., Salesforce, Outreach, HubSpot) preferred.
