Deel is building the future of work.

As part of that mission, we are building a global financial infrastructure to power payroll, payments, treasury, compliance, and accounting at scale. We already process tens of billions in annual payment volume, supporting hundreds of thousands of workers and tens of thousands of businesses worldwide.

As we move toward IPO readiness, we are doubling down on building regulated, scalable, and auditable financial systems — spanning wallets, ledgers, reconciliation, invoicing, and global payment orchestration.

We are looking for a Head of Engineering (Fintech Platform) to lead this next phase.

Role Overview

You will lead the engineering organization responsible for Deel’s end-to-end financial infrastructure, including:

  • Payments orchestration & PSP integrations

  • Wallets & regulated entity flows (FBO / safeguarding)

  • General Ledger & accounting automation

  • Treasury, FX, and liquidity systems

  • Billing, invoicing, and revenue flows

  • Reconciliation & financial data integrity

  • Fincrime & compliance systems

This role sits at the intersection of engineering, product, finance, and compliance, with direct impact on Deel’s ability to scale globally and meet IPO-grade standards.

What You’ll Do

1. Own the Fintech Engineering Vision

  • Define and execute the technical strategy for Deel’s financial infrastructure

  • Build a scalable, modular architecture across payments, ledger, treasury, and compliance

  • Drive the evolution toward real-time, event-driven financial systems

2. Lead Product-Centric Engineering

  • Partner deeply with Product, Finance, Treasury, and Compliance

  • Ensure teams deliver business outcomes, not just features

  • Embed product thinking into engineering culture in the era of LLMs

  • Drive clarity on what gets built, why, and in what order

3. Build for Scale, Accuracy, and Reliability

  • Design systems handling high transaction volumes and global scale

  • Ensure idempotency, consistency, and fault tolerance across all financial flows

  • Lead architecture for:

    • Wallet-to-wallet / wallet-to-payout flows

    • Multi-currency and FX handling

    • High-throughput payment processing

4. Deliver IPO-Grade Financial Infrastructure

Drive engineering execution across three core pillars:

  • Controls

    • SOX readiness, auditability, permissions, separation of duties

    • Strong internal controls embedded in systems

  • Compliance

    • Regulated entities (MTL, PI, EMI) across US, EU, UK, and beyond

    • KYC, AML, transaction monitoring

  • Accounting

    • Enable fast and accurate close (target 5–7 days)

    • Build and scale General Ledger and MCP (Multi-Channel Posting) infrastructure

    • Reduce manual reconciliation through automation

5. Build an AI-First Fintech Platform

Make AI a core building block of both engineering and financial systems:

AI in Engineering

  • Enable developers to leverage LLMs across the development lifecycle:

    • Code generation and refactoring

    • Automated reviews and validation

    • Detection of logical inconsistencies in financial flows

AI in Financial Systems

  • Apply AI to:

    • Reconciliation → automated matching, break detection, resolution suggestions

    • Accounting validation → detect inconsistencies before posting to financial systems

    • Transaction monitoring → anomaly detection and risk scoring

    • Data integrity → identify silent corruption or inconsistencies across systems

AI in Operations & Close

  • Build systems that:

    • Detect risks to book close timelines in real time

    • Explain discrepancies in financial data

    • Suggest or execute corrective actions

MCP Servers & AI Infrastructure

  • Design and evolve MCP server infrastructure as a foundation for:

    • AI-assisted financial workflows

    • Standardized access to financial data and services

    • Safe execution of automated or AI-driven actions

  • Ensure MCP servers enable:

    • Controlled, auditable interaction between AI systems and financial data

    • Secure orchestration of actions across payments, treasury, invoicing, and accounting

    • Clear boundaries between decision-making (AI) and execution (systems)

AI Governance & Safety

  • Ensure all AI systems are:

    • Auditable and explainable

    • Compliant with financial and regulatory requirements

    • Designed with strong guardrails for correctness and risk management

5. Drive Operational Excellence

  • Establish strong engineering processes, SLAs, and reliability standards

  • Reduce incidents impacting financial data

  • Build high-signal monitoring and observability for financial systems

  • Ensure systems are audit-ready and explainable

6. Build and Scale High-Performing Teams

  • Lead multiple teams across:

    • Payments orchestration

    • Ledger & accounting

    • Treasury

    • Billing & invoicing

    • Reconciliation

    • Fincrime

  • Hire, mentor, and develop senior engineering leaders and ICs

  • Maintain a culture of:

    • Ownership

    • Speed with control

    • High accountability

7. Drive Cross-Team Alignment

  • Align engineering across Product, Finance, Legal, and Compliance

  • Resolve cross-functional friction and unblock execution

  • Bring structure to complex, multi-team initiatives

Must Have

  • Proven experience as Head of Engineering / Director / Senior EM in fintech or financial systems

  • Built or scaled systems in companies like Stripe, Adyen, PayPal, Wise, Revolut, Coinbase, or similar

  • Deep experience with:

    • Payments infrastructure

    • Ledger/accounting systems

    • High-scale distributed systems

  • Experience in regulated environments:

    • KYC / AML

    • Compliance systems

    • Financial reporting constraints

What You Bring

Technical Leadership

  • Strong backend expertise (Node.js / TypeScript preferred, or similar stack)

  • Deep understanding of:

    • Distributed systems

    • Event-driven architectures

    • Data consistency and reconciliation

Financial Systems Understanding

  • Strong grasp of:

    • Payments and PSP

    • Double-entry accounting and General Ledger design

    • Reconciliation systems at scale

    • FX and multi-currency flows

    • Treasury operations

Execution at Scale

  • Experience scaling systems handling billions in transaction volume

  • Built systems with:

    • High concurrency

    • Idempotency guarantees

    • Strong audit trails

Product Mindset

  • Ability to translate business problems into technical solutions

  • Strong prioritization and trade-off decision-making

  • Focus on delivering real customer and business value

Leadership

  • Experience managing multiple teams and senior leaders

  • Strong communicator across technical and non-technical audiences

  • Ability to operate in high-growth, ambiguous environments

You’re a Great Fit If You

  • Think in systems, not features

  • Balance speed and control in a fintech environment

  • Are obsessed with data correctness and financial integrity

  • Can navigate complex, cross-border regulatory constraints

  • Build trust with C-suite, Finance, and Engineering teams

  • Take ownership of critical, business-impacting systems

Bonus Points

  • Experience preparing systems for IPO readiness (SOX, audit, close processes)

  • Built or operated:

    • Wallet systems

    • Payment orchestration layers

    • Treasury or liquidity platforms

    • Automated reconciliation systems

  • Experience with:

    • NetSuite or similar ERP integrations

    • Event-driven accounting systems

    • MCP / real-time posting infrastructures

Why Join

  • Build one of the most advanced global fintech stacks

  • Operate at massive scale across multiple regulated entities

  • Play a key role in Deel’s IPO journey

  • Work on problems at the intersection of:

    • Payments

    • Compliance

    • Accounting

    • Infrastructure

  • Summary

    Manager, Account Executive is responsible for leading a high-performing team of Account Executives focused on driving sales growth for a defined segment or product. This role oversees team performance, supports professional development, and ensures consistent execution across the full sales cycle. It is essential for delivering strong revenue results, improving sales effectiveness, and supporting overall commercial strategy.

    Responsibilities

    • Hire, coach, and develop a team of Account Executives, ensuring strong performance, clear expectations, and continuous skill growth.

    • Drive revenue growth by ensuring the team consistently identifies, develops, and closes opportunities for new business and/or account expansion.

    • Conduct regular 1:1s, forecast reviews, and strategy sessions to support effective planning, execution, and territory management.

    • Build and implement scalable sales processes and playbooks to support predictable, repeatable execution across the team.

    • Monitor sales pipeline, activity levels, and forecasts, proactively addressing risks and adjusting strategy as needed.

    • Support team members as a player-coach by joining key opportunities and modeling effective sales practices.

    • Partner cross-functionally with internal teams to enable smooth handoffs, strong customer engagement, and alignment on priorities.

    • Analyze performance trends and KPIs to provide insights and recommendations for improvement.

    • Ensure strong operational discipline, including accurate CRM hygiene, reporting, and forecasting.

    • Lead initiatives to improve sales processes, team efficiency, and customer experience.

    • Meet or exceed monthly and yearly team revenue targets.

    Qualifications

    • Demonstrated experience leading and developing Account Executives in a B2B sales environment.

    • Strong track record of achieving sales targets and driving results through others.

    • Ability to coach sellers through complex sales cycles and strategic account planning.

    • Strong analytical capabilities with experience using data to guide decisions, assess performance, and forecast outcomes.

    • Ability to communicate complex information in a clear and effective manner.

    • Experience building scalable processes, playbooks, and operating rhythms for sales teams.

    • Strong collaboration skills with the ability to influence cross-functional stakeholders.

    • Fully proficient in French and English

Summary

The Payroll Expert serves as a regional subject matter expert, owning end-to-end payroll execution for specific countries or regions. This role requires deep technical expertise in local payroll regulations, systems implementation, and vendor management. The Payroll Expert acts as the go-to resource for complex payroll matters while driving continuous improvement in payroll processes.

Responsibilities

  • Evaluate and select relevant software for running payroll, incorporating Deel product technology requirements

  • Ensure accurate and timely execution of payroll in your region, as well as in other trained countries

  • Prepare and reconcile internal payroll reports, ensuring compliance with local tax authorities

  • Own all critical local payroll and HR processes, including setting up involuntary deductions, supporting onboardings and offboardings of employees on record in your region

  • Schedule payroll expenses collections and reconcile collections to expenses

  • Assess and improve rapid growth processes and procedures, ensuring compliance

  • Stay informed of relevant local regulatory and policies changes, communicating and establishing key changes across teams

  • Work cross-functionally to provide expertise on local payroll and HR matters in your region

  • Answer complex payroll and HR related questions from employees and clients in your region

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Accounting, or a related field from an accredited institution

  • 5+ years of payroll and HR experience with experience in relevant country

  • Strong working knowledge of local regulations and policies related to payroll and HR

  • Ability to prioritize multiple tasks while meeting deadlines timely and accurately

  • Strong verbal and written skills to connect with clients and align with various stakeholders

  • Strong interpersonal skills, explaining complex issues at an understandable level

  • Advanced knowledge of local payroll software solutions

  • Intermediate knowledge of Microsoft Excel/Google Sheets

  • Strong proficiency in English

  • Experience managing technology and external payroll providers is an advantage

  • Experience with implementation of new payrolls on an industry-recognized payroll solution is preferred

  • Experience in multiple country jurisdictions is an advantage

  • Experience with multiple payroll technologies is an advantage

Summary

The Art Director, Web Design will guide the strategic direction, execution, and continuous evolution of our marketing website. You’ll be the glue between brand expression and performance — ensuring our web experiences are excellently crafted, technically sound, and optimized for business impact.

You will partner closely with teams looking after SEO, CRO, Marketing, Communications, Growth, Product Marketing, and Product Design teams to ensure our website not only looks amazing but drives engagement, acquisition, and conversion at scale.

Responsibilities

  • Own the website creative vision and partner to help define the roadmap in alignment with web strategy, growth goals, and technical capabilities.

  • Work daily with a web team of designers, developers, CRO/SEO specialists and external partners — fostering excellence in execution and craft.

  • Collaborate cross-functionally with stakeholders across SEO, CRO, Marketing, Comms, Growth, Product Marketing, and Product Design.

  • Develop engaging experiences that drive results as measured by shared success metrics.

  • Continuously improve UX and UI through testing, iteration, and advocating for best practices.

  • Ensure web projects are delivered on time, within scope, and aligned with both brand and business performance goals.

  • Manage and continuously develop the Website Design System together with developers, based on business needs.

  • Collaborate with others on the Brand & creative team to maintain consistency across all visual touchpoints.

  • Establish systems and processes to streamline web operations to increase project velocity, and enable self-serve at scale.

  • Translate performance insights and business needs into clear briefs and initiatives.

Qualifications

  • 9+ years of experience in web design, UX/UI, or digital marketing

  • Deep understanding of web design principles, performance marketing, SEO, CRO, and responsive development.

  • AI: Curiosity about AI and emerging technologies; experience designing AI-assisted or AI-powered product experiences or using AI tools as part of your design workflow. A portfolio that includes examples of work related to AI-powered features, AI-assisted workflows, or experiments with AI tools.

  • A portfolio showcasing both strategic thinking and high-quality execution across web and digital campaigns.

  • Proven success managing web initiatives in a high-growth SaaS or tech environment.

  • Exceptional communicator who can manage input and expectations across multiple stakeholders.

  • Strong understanding of Figma, HTML/CSS, CMS systems, and analytics platforms.

  • Adept at balancing brand integrity with data-driven decision-making.

  • Experience working in agile environments and/or with developers and marketing operations teams.

Responsibilities

  • Design, build, and maintain efficient data pipelines (ETL processes) to integrate data from various source systems into the data warehouse.

  • Develop and optimize data warehouse schemas and tables to support analytics and reporting needs.

  • Write and refine complex SQL queries and use scripting (e.g., Python) to transform and aggregate large datasets.

  • Collaborate with data analysts, data scientists, and other engineers to understand data requirements and deliver appropriate solutions.

  • Document pipeline designs, data flows, and data definitions for transparency and future reference, adhering to team standards.

Role Overview

As the Director of Data Governance, you will lead the evolution of our data ecosystem from fragmented silos into a unified, high-quality enterprise asset. You will be responsible for the strategic design, implementation, and continuous improvement of the Data Governance Framework, ensuring that data across the organization is accurate, secure, and ready for advanced AI and analytics initiatives. You will understand SOX and public company data requirements and have executed within a public company.

Strategic Leadership & Framework Development

  • Establish Governance Strategy: Define and execute Data Governance roadmap that aligns with the Enterprise Information & Governance vision.

Data Quality & Standards

  • Quality Management: Implement the Data Quality Management Procedures, establishing KPIs for data accuracy, completeness, and consistency.

Security, AI, & Risk Mitigation

  • AI Readiness: Collaborate with the CFO and Security teams to ensure data pipelines are optimized for AI applications while maintaining strict security boundaries.

  • Access Control: Work with Security to define data classification levels and manage high-risk data access policies.

Stakeholder & Culture Management

  • Stewardship Program: Recruit, train, and manage a network of Data Stewards and Data Owners across business units to ensure local accountability.

  • Data Culture: Act as an evangelist for Data as an Asset, driving a culture of data literacy and accountability throughout the organization.

  • Executive Reporting: Provide regular updates to the CFO and executive leadership on governance maturity, risk levels, and the ROI of data quality initiatives.

  • Experience with AI systems in production

  • Proven ability to draft "Decision Papers" and Charters that gain executive buy-in

  • Strong experience of DAMA-DMBOK frameworks and data quality lifecycle management

About the role

We’re looking for a Senior Staff Product Designer to lead the design vision for Deel’s AI vertical—one of the most ambitious and strategically important areas of the company. This role sits at the intersection of conversational AI, agentic workflows, and intelligent automation, shaping the experiences that define Deel’s next-generation platform.

You’ll own the end-to-end design strategy for our AI products, including Deel AI, Akai, conversational interfaces, AI-powered agents, and developer experience tools. Your scope covers everything from in-chat interactions and agent-driven workflows to proactive notifications, anomaly detection, and human-in-the-loop interventions across payroll, HR, and compliance.

This isn’t just about making AI features look good—it’s about defining how intelligence shows up across Deel’s entire ecosystem. You’ll work closely with the Principal Designer (who leads intent bar orchestration and the intelligent OS layer) to establish a seamless handoff between conversational AI, agentic actions, and the broader platform experience. Together, you’ll shape the foundational patterns that make Deel feel like one coherent, intelligent system.

You’ll lead a growing team of designers and partner directly with engineering, product, and leadership to set the direction for AI design at Deel—codifying craft standards, mentoring designers, and influencing strategy across verticals.

Key responsibilities

Lead AI design strategy

  • Define the design direction for Deel’s AI vertical, Akai, conversational interfaces, agents, labs, and developer experience tools.

  • Establish the strategic approach for how intelligence surfaces across the platform—from proactive agent notifications and anomaly detection to in-chat actions and workflow automation.

  • Shape strategic conversations using insights, narratives, and design vision to influence direction and uncover new opportunity spaces across the AI product portfolio.

  • Work in close synergy with the Principal Designer to define the boundary between conversational AI (your scope) and intent bar orchestration and the intelligent OS (their scope), ensuring seamless handoff and coherence.

Drive craft excellence across AI experiences

  • Codify craft standards for AI interactions by formalizing patterns, quality expectations, and reusable approaches that emerge from practice.

  • Ensure coherence across the AI vertical, reducing fragmentation and inconsistencies between teams building conversational, agentic, and automated experiences.

  • Turn implicit good design into explicit guidelines and mentor designers to consistently apply high-quality craft to AI products.

  • Design and prototype at speed—rapidly exploring new interaction patterns for conversational AI, agent-driven workflows, and human-in-the-loop experiences.

Own collaboration and communication

  • Drive effective cross-team collaboration within the AI vertical, fostering alignment and shared ownership across designers, engineers, product managers, and business partners.

  • Influence alignment through clear narrative and communication—managing stakeholders across teams with minimal oversight.

  • Shape shared understanding through compelling design narratives that connect AI design choices to user impact and business outcomes.

  • Partner with the design system team to ensure AI-enabled workflows, agentic UI patterns, and tool integrations are reflected in the system.

Take ownership and drive outcomes

  • Lead problem spaces within the AI vertical, setting direction and defining success for complex, ambiguous initiatives.

  • Anticipate challenges and opportunities ahead of time, creating clarity before issues materialize across DAI, agents, and conversational AI.

  • Make prioritization and trade-off decisions that unblock teams and maintain momentum across the AI product portfolio.

  • Take accountability for progress and outcomes—shipping high-quality AI experiences that move the needle for customers and the business.

Champion customer focus and research

  • Establish user research and customer insight practices within the AI vertical, ensuring empathy and evidence are embedded from problem framing through delivery.

  • Drive competitor and market analysis that shapes AI design direction and informs strategic priorities across teams.

  • Champion quality control by defining dogfooding rituals and feedback loops that surface issues early and raise the bar for AI experience quality.

  • Coach designers on research methods and ensure the voice of the customer is represented in cross-functional decisions about AI products.

Mentor and grow the team

  • Shape design org culture within the AI vertical, driving initiatives that improve how designers work and grow.

  • Invest time in coaching and supporting designers—turning successful experiments into repeatable approaches that others can build on.

  • Data-informed and evidence-driven—your design work is rooted in experimentation, user research, and measurable outcomes.

The Role

As a Presales Delivery Consultant within Deel’s Global Payroll Solutions organisation, you will play a critical role in ensuring our global payroll customers transition from Sales to Implementation with clarity, confidence, and delivery readiness.

Reporting to the Director of Pre-Sales Delivery, you will lead in-depth payroll discovery and readiness validation for Mid-Market and Enterprise customers, ensuring that Onboarding Managers (OBMs) and Project Managers (PMs) receive a complete and accurate view of each client’s payroll landscape.

You will also support Sales on complex opportunities by providing payroll and implementation expertise, helping set realistic expectations and ensuring that what is sold can be delivered successfully.

This role sits at the heart of Deel’s Sales-to-Delivery motion, protecting customer experience, accelerating time-to-value, and enabling scalable global payroll growth.

What You’ll Do:

Payroll Discovery & Readiness

  • Lead detailed payroll discovery sessions with prospective and newly signed customers

  • Gather and document country scope, payroll populations, local processes, pay frequencies, benefits, statutory obligations, and integrations

  • Identify operational, compliance, and product risks early in the customer journey

  • Validate feasibility of scope, timelines, and rollout approaches

  • Prepare and advise of go live dates and roll out plans.

Sales to Implementation Handover

  • Create comprehensive, delivery-ready handover packs for OBMs and PMs

  • Ensure no loss of knowledge or scope between Sales and Implementation

  • Clearly document payroll assumptions, risks, dependencies, and constraints

  • Prevent late-stage surprises, re-discovery, and scope creep

Sales Enablement & Deal Support

  • Support Sales teams on complex global payroll opportunities by providing payroll and delivery expertise

  • Join selected sales calls to validate scope, explain implementation requirements, and build client confidence

  • Ensure customer expectations are aligned with delivery reality

Solutions & Payroll Enablement

  • Run Country Spotlight and enablement sessions with Payroll Implementation teams

  • Share country-specific complexity, risks, and best practices

  • Feed discovery insights into Product, Implementation tooling, Sales enablement, and delivery methodology

What We’re Looking For

We’re looking for a delivery-minded payroll professional who thrives in complex, fast-paced, global environments and understands that successful payroll implementations begin with exceptional discovery.

You will bring:

  • 5+ years of experience in global payroll, payroll implementation, or payroll operations

  • Strong understanding of multi-country payroll complexity and compliance

  • Experience leading or supporting payroll discovery, onboarding, or implementation projects

  • Ability to run structured, high-impact client discovery sessions

  • Excellent documentation and handover discipline

  • Confidence to raise risks and challenge assumptions constructively

  • Strong communication skills with technical and non-technical stakeholders

  • Comfort working cross-functionally across Sales, Product, Payroll, and Implementation

  • Experience using tools such as Salesforce, Notion, and project tracking tools

About the Role

We’re looking for a strategic and technically savvy Solutions Engineer to partner closely with the Deel IT Sales Team. In this role, you'll work hand-in-hand with Account Executives and Product teams to support prospective clients as they explore Deel’s fast-growing IT product suite - ranging from global hardware procurement to Device Lifecycle Management Logistics.

You’ll be instrumental in translating complex client needs into actionable, scalable, and compliant solutions - supporting every stage of the sales process from discovery through to implementation hand-off.

What You'll Do

  • Partner with Account Executives in pre-sales cycles to uncover technical and operational pain points related to global IT equipment deployment, asset lifecycle management, and cross-border workforce support.

  • Design and present tailored IT solution packages that may include device procurement/leasing models, HRIS integrations, global device lifecycle solutions, and SLA-backed managed services.

  • Act as the technical voice in client meetings - owning demos, RFP responses, IT security reviews, and product deep-dives.

  • Translate customer workflows and requirements into proposed configurations and processes within Deel’s IT platform.

  • Advise on implementation considerations across multiple geographies, including compliance, import/export logistics, and local market nuances.

  • Collaborate with Product, Operations, Legal, and Enablement to advocate for prospect needs, accelerate deal velocity, and refine value messaging.

  • Stay ahead of evolving trends in global workforce IT support.

What You’ll Bring

  • 3+ years of experience in a client-facing Solutions Engineering, Pre-Sales, Technical Consulting, or similar role - preferably in SaaS, IT Asset Management, or HR Tech.

  • Strong understanding of IT procurement, device lifecycle management, or SaaS integrations (ITSM systems, MDM, SSO, Endpoint Security etc).

  • Comfort discussing APIs, data flows, and systems architecture with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

  • Excellent communication and storytelling skills - able to simplify complex solutions and clearly articulate ROI.

  • Strong cross-functional collaboration instincts and the ability to manage competing priorities in fast-moving sales cycles.

  • Deep analytical mindset with the ability to build excel based tools to drive client solutions.

  • Bonus: Experience supporting enterprise clients in multi-country environments.

Comptant parmi les plus grandes entreprises au monde, notre équipe de 7 000 collaborateurs s'étend sur plus de 100 pays, parle 74 langues et porte une culture connectée et dynamique qui favorise l'apprentissage continu et l'innovation pour nos clients.

En tant qu'entreprise de Logiciel en tant que Service (SaaS) à la croissance la plus rapide de l'histoire, Deel transforme la manière dont les talents mondiaux se connectent aux entreprises de premier plan ; brisant les frontières qui limitent traditionnellement l'embauche et les opportunités de carrière. Notre élan se reflète dans nos réussites et la satisfaction de nos clients : CNBC Disruptor 50, Forbes Cloud 100, Deloitte Fast 500, ainsi qu'une reconnaissance répétée dans la liste des meilleures entreprises de Y Combinator, tout en maintenant une note moyenne de 4,83 basée sur 15 000 avis via G2, Trustpilot, Capterra, Apple et Google.

Votre expérience chez Deel sera un véritable accélérateur de carrière. À l'avant-garde de la révolution mondiale du travail, vous relèverez des défis complexes qui impactent la vie professionnelle de millions de personnes. Porté par notre dynamique, soutenue par une valorisation de 17,3 milliards de dollars et un revenu annuel récurrent (ARR) d'un milliard de dollars en un peu plus de cinq ans, vous générerez un impact significatif tout en développant une expertise qui fera de vous un leader convoité de la transformation du travail à l'échelle mondiale.

À propos du poste Nous sommes à la recherche d'un(e) ingénieur(e) de solutions stratégique et technophile pour collaborer étroitement avec l'équipe des ventes de Deel IT. Dans ce rôle, vous travaillerez en étroite collaboration avec les gestionnaires de comptes et les équipes produit afin d'accompagner les clients potentiels dans l'exploration de la suite de produits TI en pleine croissance de Deel — allant de l'approvisionnement mondial en matériel informatique à la gestion du cycle de vie des appareils.

Vous jouerez un rôle déterminant dans la transformation des besoins complexes des clients en solutions concrètes, évolutives et conformes — en soutenant chaque étape du processus de vente, de la découverte jusqu'au transfert vers l'équipe d’implantation

Ce que vous ferez

  • Collaborer avec les gestionnaires de comptes lors des cycles de prévente pour cerner les points de friction techniques et opérationnels liés au déploiement mondial d'équipements TI, à la gestion du cycle de vie des actifs et au soutien des effectifs transfrontaliers.

  • Concevoir et présenter des forfaits de solutions TI sur mesure pouvant inclure des modèles d'approvisionnement ou de location d'appareils, des intégrations SIRH, des solutions mondiales de cycle de vie des appareils et des services gérés avec ententes de niveau de service (ENS).

  • Agir à titre de voix technique lors des réunions clients — en prenant en charge les démonstrations, les réponses aux appels d'offres, les revues de sécurité informatique et les présentations approfondies des produits.

  • Traduire les flux de travail et les exigences des clients en configurations et processus proposés au sein de la plateforme TI de Deel.

  • Conseiller sur les considérations de mise en œuvre dans plusieurs zones géographiques, y compris la conformité, la logistique d'importation/exportation et les particularités des marchés locaux.

  • Collaborer avec les équipes Produit, Opérations, Juridique et Habilitation pour défendre les besoins des prospects, accélérer la conclusion des ententes et affiner les messages de valeur.

  • Demeurer à l'affût des tendances émergentes en matière de soutien TI pour les effectifs mondiaux.

Ce que vous apportez

  • 3 ans ou plus d'expérience dans un rôle axé sur la clientèle en ingénierie de solutions, prévente, consultation technique ou un rôle similaire — de préférence dans le domaine du SaaS, de la gestion des actifs TI ou des RH Tech.

  • Solide compréhension de l'approvisionnement en TI, de la gestion du cycle de vie des appareils ou des intégrations SaaS (systèmes ITSM, MDM, authentification unique, sécurité des points de terminaison, etc.).

  • Aisance à discuter d'API, de flux de données et d'architecture de systèmes avec des interlocuteurs tant techniques que non techniques.

  • Excellentes aptitudes en communication et en narration — capacité à simplifier des solutions complexes et à articuler clairement le retour sur investissement.

  • Solides instincts de collaboration interfonctionnelle et capacité à gérer des priorités concurrentes dans des cycles de vente en évolution rapide.

  • Esprit analytique développé avec la capacité de créer des outils basés sur Excel pour orienter les solutions clients.

  • Atout : Expérience auprès de clients entreprises dans des environnements multinationals.

Si ce poste vous correspond, nous serions ravis de vous entendre ! Ces qualités sont essentielles pour réussir dans ce rôle, alors postulez si vous êtes enthousiaste à l'idée d'apporter ces compétences à notre équipe et de grandir avec nous. Nous accueillons des candidats de tous horizons et recherchons des personnes passionnées, prêtes à faire la différence.

Envie de vous démarquer ? Nous vous encourageons à nous prospecter ! Contactez l'équipe de recrutement pour nous informer de votre candidature, montrez-nous vos compétences en action et démontrez votre approche proactive dans la construction de relations.

Rémunération Globale

Nos collaborateurs méritent une rémunération juste et compétitive qui s'adapte à leur réalité. Avec des avantages sociaux et des gratifications évolutives, notre programme de rémunération globale reflète notre engagement en faveur de l'inclusion et de l'accessibilité pour tous.

Ce dont vous bénéficierez :

  • Des opportunités d'attribution d'actions (stock options) selon votre rôle, votre statut d'emploi et votre localisation.

  • Des avantages et privilèges supplémentaires basés sur votre statut d'emploi et votre pays.

  • La flexibilité du travail à distance, incluant un accès optionnel aux espaces WeWork.

Égalité des chances et Diversité

Chez Deel, nous sommes un employeur garantissant l'égalité des chances. Nous valorisons la diversité et encourageons vivement les candidatures de personnes qualifiées, sans distinction de race, de religion, de sexe, d'origine nationale, d'identité de genre, d'orientation sexuelle, d'âge, d'état civil, de statut de vétéran, de handicap, de grossesse ou de maternité, ou de toute autre caractéristique protégée par la loi.

Sauf accord contraire, nous communiquons avec les candidats via des adresses e-mail spécifiques à Deel (se terminant par @deel.com ou celles de sociétés acquises comme @payspace.com et @paygroup.com). Vous pouvez consulter nos offres d'emploi à jour sur notre page carrières.

Accessibilité et Processus de Recrutement

Deel s'engage à offrir des aménagements sur demande tout au long du processus de recrutement, de sélection et d'évaluation pour les candidats en situation de handicap. Si vous avez besoin d'un aménagement, veuillez en informer notre équipe d'acquisition de talents via [ce lien] ; un membre de l'équipe vous contactera pour garantir votre participation équitable. En cas de difficulté d'accès au formulaire, contactez-nous à recruiting@deel.com.

Notre processus repose principalement sur des entretiens et des évaluations liées au poste. Dans certains cas restreints, nous pouvons examiner des informations contextuelles informelles pertinentes pour le rôle, conformément à nos obligations de confidentialité et d'équité.

Utilisation de l'IA et de la Technologie

Ce processus de candidature utilise des outils d'aide à la décision automatisée (AEDT) et des systèmes d'IA pour évaluer les candidats en fonction de leur niveau d'expérience, de leurs compétences techniques et de leurs qualifications. En tant qu'entreprise 100 % en télétravail, nous utilisons également des technologies de détection de fraude et de deepfake propulsées par l'IA pour vérifier l'authenticité de l'identité des candidats lors des évaluations. Ce traitement est effectué dans le respect des lois sur la protection des données, la gouvernance de l'IA et le droit du travail. Nous garantissons qu'une supervision humaine est maintenue pour toutes les décisions finales d'embauche. Vos données personnelles ne sont pas utilisées pour entraîner des modèles d'IA. Pour plus d'informations, consultez notre Politique de Confidentialité.

job_title: Gestionnaire Senior du Marketing Payant, Sponsorships
job_description:
Who we are is what we do.

Deel is the all-in-one payroll and HR platform for global teams. Our vision is to unlock global opportunity for every person, team, and business. Built for the way the world works today, Deel combines HRIS, payroll, compliance, benefits, performance, and equipment management into one seamless platform. With AI-powered tools and a fully owned payroll infrastructure, Deel supports every worker type in 150+ countries—helping businesses scale smarter, faster, and more compliantly.

Among the largest globally distributed companies in the world, our team of 7,000 spans more than 100 countries, speaks 74 languages, and brings a connected and dynamic culture that drives continuous learning and innovation for our customers.

Why should you be part of our success story?

As the fastest-growing Software as a Service (SaaS) company in history, Deel is transforming how global talent connects with world-class companies – breaking down borders that have traditionally limited both hiring and career opportunities. We're not just building software; we're creating the infrastructure for the future of work, enabling a more diverse and inclusive global economy. In 2024 alone, we paid $11.2 billion to workers in nearly 100 currencies and provided healthcare and benefits to workers in 109 countries—ensuring people get paid and protected, no matter where they are.

Our momentum is reflected in our achievements and customer satisfaction: CNBC Disruptor 50,  Forbes Cloud 100, Deloitte Fast 500, and repeated recognition on Y Combinator’s top companies list – all while maintaining a 4.83 average rating from 15,000 reviews across G2, Trustpilot, Captera, Apple and Google.

Your experience at Deel will be a career accelerator. At the forefront of the global work revolution, you'll tackle complex challenges that impact millions of people's working lives. With our momentum—backed by a $17.3 billion valuation and $1 B in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in just over five years—you'll drive meaningful impact while building expertise that makes you a sought-after leader in the transformation of global work.

Gestionnaire Senior du Marketing Payant

Ce que vous ferez chez Deel

  • Gérer les efforts de génération de demande et de commandites auprès d’éditeurs en dehors des canaux de médias sociaux et de recherche payants. Cela inclut, sans s'y limiter, les infolettres, les publi-reportages, les balados, l’affichage (OOH), la télévision connectée (CTV) et plus encore

  • Effectuer une veille constante pour identifier de nouveaux emplacements publicitaires, éditeurs et catégories afin d’y introduire Deel, de tester les opportunités et d'itérer.

  • Gérer les budgets de campagne, analyser les programmes passés et produire des rapports sur les indicateurs de performance clés (KPI).

  • Assurer le suivi des rapports pour l'ensemble des achats et communiquer de façon proactive avec les équipes régionales et les autres leaders interfonctionnels pour garantir la visibilité de tous les programmes.

  • Établir des processus reproductibles, des modèles et des meilleures pratiques pour la gestion, la croissance et le reporting des campagnes. Agir à titre d'expert sur l'excellence opérationnelle et les opportunités de commandite chez Deel.

  • Collaborer avec nos responsables marketing locaux et nos responsables des communications pour identifier les tendances clés et les sites Web locaux pertinents pour des partenariats, en complément des partenaires mondiaux.

  • Informer l'équipe de publicité payante, l'équipe de croissance élargie et les autres gestionnaires régionaux des dernières tendances et des rapports de performance afin de proposer de nouvelles idées favorisant la croissance.

Quelques qualifications clés

  • Plus de 5 ans d'expérience dans un rôle similaire de gestion de commandites auprès d’éditeurs (une expérience comparable en B2B/SaaS est préférable).

  • Excellente connaissance et maîtrise des différents modèles d'achat (Portée, CPM, CPL, CPC, etc.) ainsi que des objectifs de performance (CPSQO, ROAS, etc.) et des opérations liées aux éditeurs de génération de demande.

  • Agilité, flexibilité et autonomie démontrées dans la gestion de projets, de la conception à la réalisation, avec diverses parties prenantes.

  • Esprit créatif et analytique capable de tester, de mettre en œuvre et de mesurer les résultats tout en proposant régulièrement de nouvelles idées.

  • Approche axée sur les données; aisance à configurer des tableaux de bord et à utiliser les outils d'analyse récents pour suivre la performance. Utilisation d’outils d'IA (tels que Claude, Lovable, Gemini) pour automatiser et accélérer les tâches manuelles et l'analyse.

  • Aptitude marquée pour le réseautage et la collaboration avec des équipes internes et externes afin d'améliorer les processus et les flux de communication.

  • Motivation personnelle, aisance dans un environnement au rythme rapide et capacité d'adaptation aux changements de structures organisationnelles.

  • Maîtrise de l'anglais, à l'oral comme à l'écrit.

  • Vaste expérience en marketing interfonctionnel dans le secteur SaaS B2B; une expérience au sein d’une startup en forte croissance ou dans les secteurs HRTech ou FinTech est un plus.

  • Expérience de travail au sein d'une startup à croissance très rapide.

  • Expérience préalable réussie au sein d'une équipe à distance et distribuée.

  • Maîtrise d'autres langues en plus de l'anglais.

With our momentum—backed by a $17.3 billion valuation and $1 B in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in just over five years—you'll drive meaningful impact while building expertise that makes you a sought-after leader in the transformation of global work.

Global Payroll Solutions

The Role

As a Presales Delivery Consultant within Deel’s Global Payroll Solutions organization, you will play a critical role in ensuring our global payroll customers transition from Sales to Implementation with clarity, confidence, and delivery readiness.

Reporting to the Director of Pre-Sales Delivery, you will lead in-depth payroll discovery and readiness validation for Mid-Market and Enterprise customers, ensuring that Onboarding Managers (OBMs) and Project Managers (PMs) receive a complete and accurate view of each client’s payroll landscape.

You will also support Sales on complex opportunities by providing payroll and implementation expertise, helping set realistic expectations and ensuring that what is sold can be delivered successfully.

This role sits at the heart of Deel’s Sales-to-Delivery motion, protecting customer experience, accelerating time-to-value, and enabling scalable global payroll growth.

What You’ll Do

Payroll Discovery & Readiness

  • Lead detailed payroll discovery sessions with prospective and newly signed customers

  • Gather and document country scope, payroll populations, local processes, pay frequencies, benefits, statutory obligations, and integrations

  • Identify operational, compliance, and product risks early in the customer journey

  • Validate feasibility of scope, timelines, and rollout approaches

  • Prepare and advise of go live dates and roll out plans.

Sales to Implementation Handover

  • Create comprehensive, delivery-ready handover packs for OBMs and PMs

  • Ensure no loss of knowledge or scope between Sales and Implementation

  • Clearly document payroll assumptions, risks, dependencies, and constraints

  • Prevent late-stage surprises, re-discovery, and scope creep

Sales Enablement & Deal Support

  • Support Sales teams on complex global payroll opportunities by providing payroll and delivery expertise

  • Join selected sales calls to validate scope, explain implementation requirements, and build client confidence

  • Ensure customer expectations are aligned with delivery reality

Solutions & Payroll Enablement

  • Run Country Spotlight and enablement sessions with Payroll Implementation teams

  • Share country-specific complexity, risks, and best practices

  • Feed discovery insights into Product, Implementation tooling, Sales enablement, and delivery methodology

What We’re Looking For

We’re looking for a delivery-minded payroll professional who thrives in complex, fast-paced, global environments and understands that successful payroll implementations begin with exceptional discovery.

You will bring:

  • 5+ years of experience in global payroll, payroll implementation, or payroll operations

  • Strong understanding of multi-country payroll complexity and compliance

  • Experience leading or supporting payroll discovery, onboarding, or implementation projects

  • Ability to run structured, high-impact client discovery sessions

  • Excellent documentation and handover discipline

  • Confidence to raise risks and challenge assumptions constructively

  • Strong communication skills with technical and non-technical stakeholders

  • Comfort working cross-functionally across Sales, Product, Payroll, and Implementation

  • Experience using tools such as Salesforce, Notion, and project tracking tools

  • Fluency in English (additional languages a plus)

Solutions de paie mondiale

Le rôle:

En tant que Consultant(e) en livraison prévente au sein de l'organisation Solutions de paie mondiale de Deel, vous jouerez un rôle essentiel pour assurer la transition de nos clients en paie mondiale, de la Vente à la Mise en œuvre, avec clarté, confiance et une préparation à la livraison optimale.

Relevant du Directeur de la livraison prévente, vous dirigerez des séances approfondies de découverte et de validation de l'état de préparation en matière de paie pour les clients du marché intermédiaire et du marché Entreprise, en veillant à ce que les Gestionnaires d'intégration (OBM) et les Chefs de projet (PM) disposent d'une vue complète et précise du paysage de paie de chaque client.

Vous appuierez également l'équipe des Ventes dans le cadre d'opportunités complexes en apportant votre expertise en paie et en mise en œuvre, en contribuant à établir des attentes réalistes et en vous assurant que ce qui est vendu peut être livré avec succès.

Ce rôle est au cœur du processus Vente-Livraison de Deel, protégeant l'expérience client, accélérant le délai de mise en valeur et favorisant une croissance évolutive de la paie mondiale.

Ce que vous ferez

Découverte et préparation en matière de paie

  • Diriger des séances détaillées de découverte de la paie avec les clients potentiels et les clients nouvellement signés

  • Recueillir et documenter la portée par pays, les effectifs de paie, les processus locaux, les fréquences de paie, les avantages sociaux, les obligations légales et les intégrations

  • Identifier les risques opérationnels, de conformité et de produit tôt dans le parcours client

  • Valider la faisabilité de la portée, des échéanciers et des approches de déploiement

  • Préparer et conseiller sur les dates de mise en service et les plans de déploiement

Transfert de la Vente à la Mise en œuvre

  • Créer des dossiers de transfert complets et prêts à la livraison pour les OBM et les PM

  • Assurer l'absence de perte de connaissances ou de portée entre les Ventes et la Mise en œuvre

  • Documenter clairement les hypothèses, les risques, les dépendances et les contraintes liés à la paie

  • Prévenir les surprises en phase avancée, la redécouverte et le dépassement de portée

Soutien aux ventes et aux opportunités

  • Appuyer les équipes des Ventes dans le cadre d'opportunités complexes en paie mondiale en apportant une expertise en paie et en livraison

  • Participer à certains appels de vente pour valider la portée, expliquer les exigences de mise en œuvre et renforcer la confiance des clients

  • Assurer l'alignement des attentes des clients avec la réalité de la livraison

Habilitation en solutions et en paie

  • Animer des séances Country Spotlight et des séances d'habilitation avec les équipes de mise en œuvre de la paie

  • Partager les complexités, les risques et les meilleures pratiques propres à chaque pays

  • Intégrer les données de découverte dans les outils de Produit, de Mise en œuvre, d'habilitation des Ventes et de méthodologie de livraison

Ce que nous recherchons

Nous recherchons un(e) professionnel(le) de la paie axé(e) sur la livraison, qui s'épanouit dans des environnements complexes, dynamiques et mondiaux, et qui comprend que la réussite des mises en œuvre de paie commence par une découverte exceptionnelle.

Vous apporterez :

  • 5 ans ou plus d'expérience en paie mondiale, en mise en œuvre de la paie ou en opérations de paie

  • Une solide compréhension de la complexité et de la conformité en matière de paie multi-pays

  • De l'expérience à diriger ou à soutenir des projets de découverte, d'intégration ou de mise en œuvre de la paie

  • La capacité d'animer des séances de découverte client structurées et à fort impact

  • Une excellente discipline en matière de documentation et de transfert

  • La confiance nécessaire pour soulever des risques et remettre en question les hypothèses de façon constructive

  • De solides compétences en communication avec des intervenants techniques et non techniques

  • L'aisance à travailler de façon transversale avec les équipes des Ventes, du Produit, de la Paie et de la Mise en œuvre

  • De l'expérience avec des outils tels que Salesforce, Notion et des outils de suivi de projets

  • La maîtrise de l'anglais (la connaissance de langues supplémentaires est un atout)

Grâce à des outils propulsés par l'IA et une infrastructure de paie interne, Deel accompagne tous les types de travailleurs dans plus de 150 pays, aidant ainsi les entreprises à se développer de manière plus intelligente, plus rapide et plus conforme.

Comptant parmi les plus grandes entreprises au monde, notre équipe de 7 000 collaborateurs s'étend sur plus de 100 pays, parle 74 langues et porte une culture connectée et dynamique qui favorise l'apprentissage continu et l'innovation pour nos clients.

En tant qu'entreprise de Logiciel en tant que Service (SaaS) à la croissance la plus rapide de l'histoire, Deel transforme la manière dont les talents mondiaux se connectent aux entreprises de premier plan ; brisant les frontières qui limitent traditionnellement l'embauche et les opportunités de carrière.   • Identifier et générer des revenus par la vente incitative et la vente croisée auprès des clients existants

  • Gérer de façon proactive un portefeuille de comptes, incluant les relations avec les décideurs

  • Conseiller les clients existants sur leurs processus internes afin de favoriser le changement et d'élargir l'utilisation des produits Deel

  • Diriger des bilans d'affaires et mettre en œuvre des plans visant à maximiser le potentiel de revenus et à augmenter les taux de renouvellement

  • 4 ans ou plus d'expérience en vente ou en gestion de comptes

The Role

Deel IT is  redefining the way organizations oversee their remote and hybrid workplaces. This is an opportunity to be a part of a pioneering tech startup that offers the best-of-breed SaaS solutions when it comes to the deployment/lifecycle management of Office and IT equipment to just about anywhere on the planet.

We are scaling up our IT offering to provide our customers with dedicated, round-the-clock support, every day of the year. From MDM to device pre-configuration, our premium customer experience is revolutionizing the MSP industry. If you're ready for a challenge, we are expanding our exceptionally talented team with ambitious growth targets over the next two years.

We have global coverage in delivering office equipment and we want to do the same for our IT support function which is why we’re now looking for an Senior IT Support Engineer.

Requirements:

As an Senior IT Support Engineer, You will:

  • Work as part of an expert team of subject matter experts to help achieve device and IT services growth objectives.

  • Build, develop, and enhance the Deel IT device management services for sale to a global customer base as well as our IT support services offering.

  • Provide technical support for our Sales team and customer base.

  • You will be deploying, configuring, and refreshing devices for Deel IT .


You identify with the following:

  • Solutions Hunter: You are a natural problem solver, and work hard and creatively to find solutions for customer or business challenges.

  • Team Player: You work well as part of a remote team, but can also carry independent responsibility.

  • Technically minded: You have solid technical knowledge and are proactive about developing your skill set.

  • Competent communicator: You are a great communicator who can break down technical problems and communicate solutions to non-experts.

  • Methodical and focused: You work methodically and prioritize well to ensure timelines are met and milestones are reached.

What you need to succeed:

This role will suit someone who already has a keen interest in multiple areas in IT and is looking for a place to combine their experience in one role. You will be a great fit if you have:

  • Experience working with and managing a Mac & Windows estate.

  • Interest in working with a mobile device management platform (MDM) for managing a mixed device estate, such as - Jamf, Intune, or Jumpcloud.

  • Interest in application packaging and deployment for both Mac & Windows.

  • Good working knowledge of IT security.

  • Demonstrate excellent interpersonal, organizational and communication skills, with the ability to work under pressure.


Bonus Experience

  • IT experience in providing technical support to 3rd-party customers and internal end-users.

  • Some experience automating device actions using Python, Bash/ZSH & Powershell.

  • Good Windows experience with knowledge of Windows Autopilot.

Summary

The Senior Solutions Engineer (Consultant) partners directly with Deel’s go-to-market and internal teams to drive customer success. In this role, you’ll work hand-in-hand with Account Executives to support prospective clients as they evaluate Deel’s platform and product offerings—playing an essential part in showcasing value, credibility, and technical fit.

You’ll be instrumental in translating complex client needs into actionable, scalable, and compliant solutions—supporting every stage of the sales process from discovery through to implementation hand-off.


Responsibilities

  • Work directly with our go-to-market teams to showcase the value of Deel’s platform and products to deliver technical wins.

  • Consult with prospective customers to assess their current processes and tooling, identify gaps/opportunities, and propose optimized future-state solutions.

  • Act as the technical voice during sales cycles — owning solution discovery, RFP responses, platform demonstrations, and integration/architecture workshops.

  • Translate customer requirements into solution designs and implementation recommendations, aligning stakeholders on scope, approach, and success criteria.

  • Partner with Product, Engineering, and Operations to advocate for prospect needs, unblock technical questions, accelerate deal velocity, and refine value messaging.

  • Constructive disruption — challenge the status quo, always looking for opportunities to scale, improve, and refine processes, assets, and how we communicate value.

  • Develop and maintain technical enablement assets (demo narratives, discovery frameworks, reference architectures, FAQs, and competitive positioning).

  • Support Partnership and Marketing-led initiatives (webinars, events, content, and joint solution workshops), as needed.

  • Stay up-to-date on relevant technology trends, integration patterns, security/compliance expectations, and best practices to maintain strong solution credibility.

Qualifications

  • Minimum of 4 years of experience as a Solutions Engineer / Pre-Sales Consultant (or equivalent customer-facing technical role).

  • Experience supporting complex B2B sales cycles (mid-market and/or enterprise), including discovery, demos, security reviews, and integrations.

  • Strong understanding of SaaS product delivery and common integration approaches (APIs, SSO/SAML, HRIS/ERP/Finance systems, data flows).

  • Comfortable leading technical conversations with a wide range of stakeholders (IT, Security, Ops, Finance, HR, and business leaders).

  • Willingness to travel inside and outside of territory (~10% or less).

  • Outstanding presentational skills, always focusing on value (multilingual capability is a bonus)

  • Entrepreneurial DNA with a positive, can-do attitude

  • A collaborative, team player is essential

  • Comfortable in a start-up, fast-moving environment

The Observability Engineer will own the design, implementation, and evolution of our monitoring and observability ecosystem across our cloud-native SaaS platform. This role is responsible for ensuring high system reliability, performance visibility, and cost-efficient monitoring at scale. The position blends hands-on Kubernetes and AWS expertise with deep knowledge of metrics, logs, traces, and SLO-driven observability practices. The ideal candidate is a proactive problem-solver who can design scalable observability architectures, operate self-hosted monitoring stacks, optimize monitoring costs, and integrate observability into CI/CD workflows to enable resilient, high-performing production systems.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design, implement, and maintain scalable observability solutions for cloud-native environments

  • Own monitoring across AWS and Kubernetes (EKS) environments, covering clusters and workloads

  • Operate and maintain self-hosted monitoring stacks (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, Mimir, Loki, Tempo)

  • Manage and optimize DataDog (metrics, logs, APM, alerts, cost monitoring)

  • Improve observability architecture to support high availability, scalability, and fault tolerance

  • Implement monitoring cost optimization strategies (log/trace sampling, retention policies, storage optimization)

  • Automate observability infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Helm, etc.)

  • Integrate monitoring and alerting into CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions is an advantage)

  • Support capacity planning and performance tuning initiatives

  • Collaborate with DevOps, SRE, and Engineering teams to embed observability best practices

  • Drive continuous improvement of monitoring standards, tooling, and reliability practices

Required Skills & Experience

  • 5+ years of hands-on experience in monitoring / observability engineering within cloud-native environments

  • Strong experience with AWS services 5+ years of hands-on experience working with Kubernetes

  • Solid knowledge of Kubernetes monitoring, including metrics, logs, and traces for clusters and workloads, alerting, SLOs, SLIs, and dashboards.

  • Proven experience operating and maintaining self-hosted monitoring stacks, advantage: Prometheus, Grafana, Mimir, Loki, Tempo Experience designing or improving observability architectures at scale

  • Experience with DataDog (metrics, logs, APM, alerts, and cost monitoring)

  • Strong understanding of high availability, scalability, and fault-tolerant architectures

  • Experience with monitoring cost optimization, including log and trace sampling strategies, storage and retention optimization

  • Ability to automate monitoring tasks using Infrastructure as Code and scripting (Terraform, Helm, etc.)

  • Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and integrating monitoring into deployment workflows (GitHub Actions is an advantage).

  • Experience with capacity planning and performance tuning

Soft Skills

  • Strong problem-solving and analytical skills

  • Ability to work independently and take ownership of complex systems

  • Good communication skills, able to collaborate with DevOps, SRE, and other teams

  • Proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement

Summary

The Sales Development Representative plays a critical role in driving top-of-funnel growth across a defined segment or product. This role is responsible for identifying, engaging, and qualifying prospective customers through inbound and outbound prospecting. By uncovering client needs, educating prospects, and generating qualified opportunities, the SDR directly contributes to pipeline development and overall revenue performance. The position requires strong communication skills, curiosity, and the ability to operate in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

Responsibilities

  • Generate new business opportunities through inbound and outbound prospecting, including email, phone, social channels, events, and campaigns.

  • Identify, research, and qualify prospective clients by understanding their needs, challenges, and readiness to engage with sales.

  • Create and execute outreach sequences, including personalized email campaigns and cold-calling to map and penetrate accounts.

  • Engage existing customers as needed to identify cross-sell or expansion opportunities across a defined segment or product (as applicable to the team).

  • Educate and nurture leads to progress them toward a qualified meeting or next step in the buyer journey.

  • Maintain accurate and up-to-date lead and activity data in CRM and sales engagement tools.

  • Collaborate with Account Executives to ensure smooth and effective handoff of qualified opportunities.

  • Partner with Sales, Marketing, and other teams to continuously refine lead-generation processes, messaging, and workflow improvements.

  • Achieve or exceed monthly and quarterly targets for qualified meetings, Sales Qualified Opportunities, and pipeline contribution.

  • Participate in ongoing training, coaching, and skill development.

Qualifications

  • Experience in a sales development, business development, or outbound commercial role (experience level may vary depending on team needs).

  • Fully proficient in Finnish language.

  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage prospects across multiple channels.

  • Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, dynamic, and remote-friendly environment.

  • Highly organized, self-motivated, and able to manage multiple priorities and outreach activities simultaneously.

  • Proven track record of meeting or exceeding activity and pipeline-generation targets.

  • Comfortable working autonomously, taking initiative, and sourcing solutions independently.

  • Experience with CRM systems and sales engagement tools is a plus.

  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a strong growth orientation; adaptable to changes in process, product, or market focus.

  • Strong written communication skills, including the ability to craft compelling outreach messages and campaign sequences.

  • Experience in HR tech, fintech, or high-growth startup environments is beneficial but not required.

Global Payroll Solutions

The Role

As a Presales Delivery Consultant within Deel’s Global Payroll Solutions organisation, you will play a critical role in ensuring our global payroll customers transition from Sales to Implementation with clarity, confidence, and delivery readiness.

Reporting to the Director of Pre-Sales Delivery, you will lead in-depth payroll discovery and readiness validation for Mid-Market and Enterprise customers, ensuring that Onboarding Managers (OBMs) and Project Managers (PMs) receive a complete and accurate view of each client’s payroll landscape.

You will also support Sales on complex opportunities by providing payroll and implementation expertise, helping set realistic expectations and ensuring that what is sold can be delivered successfully.

This role sits at the heart of Deel’s Sales-to-Delivery motion, protecting customer experience, accelerating time-to-value, and enabling scalable global payroll growth.

What You’ll Do:

Payroll Discovery & Readiness

  • Lead detailed payroll discovery sessions with prospective and newly signed customers

  • Gather and document country scope, payroll populations, local processes, pay frequencies, benefits, statutory obligations, and integrations

  • Identify operational, compliance, and product risks early in the customer journey

  • Validate feasibility of scope, timelines, and rollout approaches

  • Prepare and advise of go live dates and roll out plans.

Sales to Implementation Handover

  • Create comprehensive, delivery-ready handover packs for OBMs and PMs

  • Ensure no loss of knowledge or scope between Sales and Implementation

  • Clearly document payroll assumptions, risks, dependencies, and constraints

  • Prevent late-stage surprises, re-discovery, and scope creep

Sales Enablement & Deal Support

  • Support Sales teams on complex global payroll opportunities by providing payroll and delivery expertise

  • Join selected sales calls to validate scope, explain implementation requirements, and build client confidence

  • Ensure customer expectations are aligned with delivery reality

Solutions & Payroll Enablement

  • Run Country Spotlight and enablement sessions with Payroll Implementation teams

  • Share country-specific complexity, risks, and best practices

  • Feed discovery insights into Product, Implementation tooling, Sales enablement, and delivery methodology

What We’re Looking For

We’re looking for a delivery-minded payroll professional who thrives in complex, fast-paced, global environments and understands that successful payroll implementations begin with exceptional discovery.

You will bring:

  • 5+ years of experience in global payroll, payroll implementation, or payroll operations

  • Strong understanding of multi-country payroll complexity and compliance

  • Experience leading or supporting payroll discovery, onboarding, or implementation projects

  • Ability to run structured, high-impact client discovery sessions

  • Excellent documentation and handover discipline

  • Confidence to raise risks and challenge assumptions constructively

  • Strong communication skills with technical and non-technical stakeholders

  • Comfort working cross-functionally across Sales, Product, Payroll, and Implementation

  • Experience using tools such as Salesforce, Notion, and project tracking tools

  • Fluency in English (additional languages a plus)

We are seeking an Information Security Engineer to serve as a technical leader in designing, implementing, and optimizing our core security platforms. This role is crucial for advancing our defense-in-depth capabilities, ensuring adherence to global regulatory frameworks, and protecting sensitive data within our remote-first, global environment. The ideal candidate will own the entire lifecycle of multiple security solutions, translating best practices and compliance requirements into actionable technical controls.

Responsibilities:

  • Act as the Subject Matter Expert (SME) for Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) tools/process including optimizing configurations/policies, developing custom threat detection rules, and proactively improving Deel’s overall security posture for remote endpoints (Mac and Windows) and cloud assets (Eg, VMs).

  • Configure, manage, and tune the full suite of security policies within SWG, CASB and ZTNA. Assist our remote colleague with seamless experience through troubleshooting end user issues as needed.

  • Continuously improve SaaS security posture with SSPM tools and processes around it. Collaborate with diverse application owners, understand security control and resolve configuration drifts for our wide range of SaaS applications from baseline.

  • Develop a rigorous review, approval, policy enforcement and auditing for browser extensions, third party OAuth applications for SaaS applications such as Google Workspace, GitHub, Jira etc to meet security and privacy standards.

  • Design, implement, and audit security policies related to enterprise browsers (Eg, Island, Chrome Enterprise etc) including controlling access to sensitive web apps, and data loss prevention (DLP) configuration, ZTNA, secure web browsing experience but not limited to.

  • Manage DLP policies across Endpoint, Network, SaaS Applications and Cloud assets, directly supporting the "Crawl-Walk-Run" program phases. Ensure DLP policies meet diverse data sovereignty, privacy (GDPR, CCPA, etc.), and national regulatory requirements.

  • Run vulnerability management programs for endpoints and servers and ensure they are patched according to the policy in collaboration with stakeholders.

Job Requirements:

  • 5+ years of experience in cybersecurity

  • Handson experience with security tools like EDR, SWG, CASB, ZTNA and DLP

  • Familiarity with cybersecurity frameworks and best practices, such as MITRE, NIST, CIS, ISO27001, SOC and others.

  • Experience in collaborating with internal stakeholders to rollout enterprise security solutions.

  • Understanding and having the ability to utilize Cloud platforms such as AWS, GCP, Azure to host security tooling. Having experience in utilizing SIEM to query security telemetry is a plus.

  • Experience managing secure email gateway is a plus.

  • Excellent English in both verbal and written.

Advantages

CISSP is good to have

Who we are is what we do.

Your experience at Deel will be a career accelerator. At the forefront of the global work revolution, you'll tackle complex challenges that impact millions of people's working lives. With our momentum—backed by a $17.3 billion valuation and $1 B in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in just over five years—you'll drive meaningful impact while building expertise that makes you a sought-after leader in the transformation of global work.

We are seeking an Information Security Engineer to serve as a technical leader in designing, implementing, and optimizing our core security platforms. This role is crucial for advancing our defense-in-depth capabilities, ensuring adherence to global regulatory frameworks, and protecting sensitive data within our remote-first, global environment. The ideal candidate will own the entire lifecycle of multiple security solutions, translating best practices and compliance requirements into actionable technical controls.

Responsibilities:

  • Act as the Subject Matter Expert (SME) for Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) tools/process including optimizing configurations/policies, developing custom threat detection rules, and proactively improving Deel’s overall security posture for remote endpoints (Mac and Windows) and cloud assets (Eg, VMs).

  • Configure, manage, and tune the full suite of security policies within SWG, CASB and ZTNA. Assist our remote colleague with seamless experience through troubleshooting end user issues as needed.

  • Continuously improve SaaS security posture with SSPM tools and processes around it. Collaborate with diverse application owners, understand security control and resolve configuration drifts for our wide range of SaaS applications from baseline.

  • Develop a rigorous review, approval, policy enforcement and auditing for browser extensions, third party OAuth applications for SaaS applications such as Google Workspace, GitHub, Jira etc to meet security and privacy standards.

  • Design, implement, and audit security policies related to enterprise browsers (Eg, Island, Chrome Enterprise etc) including controlling access to sensitive web apps, and data loss prevention (DLP) configuration, ZTNA, secure web browsing experience but not limited to.

  • Manage DLP policies across Endpoint, Network, SaaS Applications and Cloud assets, directly supporting the "Crawl-Walk-Run" program phases. Ensure DLP policies meet diverse data sovereignty, privacy (GDPR, CCPA, etc.), and national regulatory requirements.

  • Run vulnerability management programs for endpoints and servers and ensure they are patched according to the policy in collaboration with stakeholders.

Job Requirements:

  • 5+ years of experience in cybersecurity

  • Handson experience with security tools like EDR, SWG, CASB, ZTNA and DLP

  • Familiarity with cybersecurity frameworks and best practices, such as MITRE, NIST, CIS, ISO27001, SOC and others.

  • Experience in collaborating with internal stakeholders to rollout enterprise security solutions.

  • Understanding and having the ability to utilize Cloud platforms such as AWS, GCP, Azure to host security tooling. Having experience in utilizing SIEM to query security telemetry is a plus.

  • Experience managing secure email gateway is a plus.

  • Excellent English in both verbal and written.

  • CISSP is a plus

Nous recherchons un Ingénieur en Sécurité de l'Information pour occuper un rôle de leader technique dans la conception, la mise en œuvre et l'optimisation de nos plateformes de sécurité principales. Ce poste est essentiel pour renforcer nos capacités de défense en profondeur, garantir la conformité aux réglementations mondiales et protéger les données sensibles dans notre environnement entièrement à distance et international. Le candidat idéal prendra en charge l'ensemble du cycle de vie de plusieurs solutions de sécurité, en traduisant les bonnes pratiques et les exigences de conformité en contrôles techniques concrets.

Responsabilités:

  • Agir en tant qu'Expert en la Matière (SME) pour les outils et processus de Détection et Réponse (EDR), notamment en optimisant les configurations et politiques, en développant des règles de détection des menaces personnalisées, et en améliorant de manière proactive la posture de sécurité globale de Deel pour les utilisateurs distants (Mac et Windows) et les ressources cloud (ex. : machines virtuelles).

  • Configurer, gérer et affiner l'ensemble des politiques de sécurité au sein des solutions SWG, CASB et ZTNA. Accompagner nos collaborateurs à distance en résolvant les problèmes des utilisateurs finaux pour assurer une expérience fluide.

  • Améliorer en continu la posture de sécurité SaaS grâce aux outils SSPM et aux processus associés. Collaborer avec les différents propriétaires d'applications, comprendre les contrôles de sécurité et résoudre les dérives de configuration par rapport aux référentiels de nos applications SaaS.

  • Mettre en place un processus rigoureux de révision, d'approbation, d'application des politiques et d'audit pour les extensions de navigateur et les applications OAuth tierces des applications SaaS telles que Google Workspace, GitHub, Jira, etc., afin de répondre aux exigences de sécurité et de confidentialité.

  • Concevoir, mettre en œuvre et auditer les politiques de sécurité relatives aux navigateurs d'entreprise (ex. : Island, Chrome Enterprise, etc.), notamment le contrôle de l'accès aux applications web sensibles, la configuration de la prévention des pertes de données (DLP), le ZTNA et une expérience de navigation web sécurisée, entre autres.

  • Gérer les politiques DLP sur les endpoints, le réseau, les applications SaaS et les ressources cloud, en soutenant directement les phases du programme « Crawl-Walk-Run ». Veiller à ce que les politiques DLP répondent aux exigences variées en matière de souveraineté des données, de confidentialité (RGPD, CCPA, etc.) et de réglementations nationales.

  • Piloter les programmes de gestion des vulnérabilités pour les endpoints et les serveurs, et s'assurer qu'ils sont corrigés conformément à la politique définie, en collaboration avec les parties prenantes.

Profil recherché

  • 5 ans d'expérience ou plus en cybersécurité

  • Expérience pratique des outils de sécurité tels que EDR, SWG, CASB, ZTNA et DLP

  • Maîtrise des référentiels et bonnes pratiques en cybersécurité (MITRE, NIST, CIS, ISO 27001, SOC, etc.)

  • Expérience dans la collaboration avec des parties prenantes internes pour le déploiement de solutions de sécurité en entreprise

  • Compréhension des plateformes cloud (AWS, GCP, Azure) et capacité à les utiliser pour héberger des outils de sécurité. Une expérience avec un SIEM pour l'analyse de la télémétrie de sécurité est un atout.

  • Une expérience dans la gestion d'une passerelle e-mail sécurisée est un atout

  • Excellent niveau d'anglais, à l'oral comme à l'écrit

Atout

  • La certification CISSP est appréciée
